The worst thing here is parking, paid garage run by Metropolis. Also outlet access isn’t great if you bring your laptop, so come charged up.

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: 4 PM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 4 PM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 4 PM - 10 PM", "Friday: 1 PM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 12 PM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 7 PM"]
Description: Craft brewery taproom in South End pouring a rotating lineup of beers on draft, with additional bar options like cocktails. The space is positioned as dog- and family-friendly, with a rooftop patio known for skyline views and a relaxed hangout vibe. Food is typically available on site through a resident food option, making it easy to settle in for a longer visit. Good for casual meetups, weekend hangs, and post-work stops.
Comments:
Free Wi-Fi, great upstairs patio with a view of Charlotte’s skyline. Industrial chic aesthetic. Friendly staff, and there’s a coffee shop within, Cool Idiot Coffee, making this a solid place to hangout. Access to outlets is solid downstairs by the taller tables.

Their parking lot is free but super small, in the vicinity there’s some free parallel parking, some paid of the same variety, and some paid lots. So you’ll just have to float around and find where you fit.

Hours: ["Monday: 7:30 AM - 3 PM", "Tuesday: 7:30 AM - 3 PM", "Wednesday: 7:30 AM - 3 PM", "Thursday: 7:30 AM - 3 PM", "Friday: 7:30 AM - 3 PM", "Saturday: 8:30 AM - 3 PM", "Sunday: 8:30 AM - 3 PM"]
Description: Compact coffee counter located inside HopFly Brewing in Charlotte’s South End, near the stairwell. The menu centers on espresso drinks alongside matcha, loose-leaf tea, hand-squeezed lemonade, and pastries. It works well as a quick caffeine stop, or as an add-on when you want a coffee while hanging out in the larger taproom it sits within.
Comments:
This spot is inside HopFly Brewing, which means there’s plenty of seating and space to spread out.  My favorite area is upstairs, there’s a side room area with some lounge chairs that face South Mint Street and then a patio with great views of the Charlotte skyline. Nice lowkey spot to hangout in an otherwise busy neighborhood.

The coffee shop itself has its own counter behind the breweries main counter downstairs. The menu is expansive, with house made syrups, all sorts of coffee options, some pastries (no cinnamon rolls but they’ve got some other nice options), herbal teas, and more. HopFly Brewing aesthetically is pretty standard Charlotte brewery, some variation of industrial chic, but I like it, they play good music. 

Free Wi-Fi so this is also a solid work/study option, just bring noise canceling headphones. Access to outlets is good downstairs by the taller tables. Parking is the biggest challenge here. There are a few free spots in a small parking lot, but it’s usually full, so it’s street parking from there that’s a mix of free parallel and some paid lots nearby.

Address: 618 W Franklin Blvd Suite D, Gastonia, NC 28052
Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 9 PM"]
Description:
A flagship "Exquisite" location from Waterbean Coffee, a local chain with 20 locations across the Charlotte region. The "Exquisite" concept is Waterbean's premium format, distinguished from their standard shops by the addition of a cocktail bar, live piano, Vietnam-inspired interior design, and an expanded food menu. The centerpiece is a wrap-around bar with espresso preparation on one side and cocktails on the other, all inside a 4,000+ square foot space in Gastonia's Fuse District.

The menu goes well beyond typical coffee shop fare, covering USDA-certified organic pour-over, siphon, Vietnamese coffee, espresso tasting flights, cocktails, loose leaf tea, and food items like the Dubai Chocolate Croissant and Ribeye Melt. Open daily until 9 PM, it serves as a comfortable spot for remote work, studying, or socializing.
Comments:
So, when I first moved to Charlotte, I had a few natives make comments to me about Gastonia that painted it like the elephant graveyard from The Lion King, a whole "you must never go there" sort of vibe. I'm happy to report that couldn't be further from the truth. Gastonia is fine, it has its own hidden gems, and this spot is certainly one of them. Shout out to Ty C. for putting me on to it!

This is Waterbean's first "Exquisite" location, which is basically their step-up concept. The difference from a regular Waterbean is that you get cocktails, live piano, and a design that pulls from their Vietnam location. And it does feel exquisite. It's a noticeably different vibe from walking into one of their standard shops.

To start, it's huge. Just the main area alone could seat well over 40 people but then there's a side room sort of spot that can fit about 20 more. Finding seating should never be a problem coming here, same with parking, plenty of space. They have all the menu items you've come to expect from a modern coffee shop, but also food (the Waterbean Special sandwich is great), and really good loose leaf herbal teas. The Candied Orange Green tea is great.

In terms of the aesthetic, it's polished. White faux marble tables, really comfortable black plush chairs, and a lot of accent lights all around to keep the place bright and energetic. It's an excellent spot to work remotely, great outlet access, just bring some noise cancelling headphones as they do play background music. If you really want to focus, go into the side room they have across from the checkout counter. It's open to all unless otherwise stated, sometimes it's booked for an event.

Address: 14835 Ballantyne Village Way Suite 130, Charlotte, NC 28277
Hours: ["Monday: 10 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 10 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 10 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 10 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 10 AM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 10 AM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 10 PM"]
Description: A dessert-focused ice cream shop in Ballantyne Village serving handcrafted gelato, artisanal chocolates, pastries, and espresso-based coffee drinks. The menu emphasizes variety, including dairy-free, vegan, and no-sugar-added options, with rotating featured flavors and occasional seasonal callouts. The space supports casual lingering with tables and tabletop games that make it practical for small groups or families. Hours run later than many dessert spots in the area, which makes it a useful evening option after dinner nearby.
Comments:
Credit to CATHZINE on Substack and Instagram for putting me on to this spot as a third place. It's more than just a grab and go ice cream spot, as they have free Wi-Fi, a good amount of comfortable seating, plenty of parking, outlets, and friendly staff.

They’re open pretty late most days, so you can camp out here in the evening with your laptop, or with friends, and you'll usually be one of the few, as it's more popular during the day. There's coffee, tea, pastries, but no food, so don't come hungry. Still, this is a great low key spot to hang out. The owners sweet and gentle French bulldog hangs around here a lot. There’s usually a ball hanging around you can use to play fetch, or you can just pet him/her (couldn’t tell)! 

I’d say this place is great for chilling and reading a book, or meeting friends, or just sitting down to eat ice cream and stare at the very nicely adorned walls. They have Wi-Fi so you could work from here, but the vibe feels more relax, stay awhile, and leave work behind. Last tip, there’s a parking garage for the complex on the back side, so head there if you can’t find parking out front.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Tuesday: Closed", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 3 PM"]
Description: Authentic artisan French bakery and cafe in Cornelius serving a pastry case full of laminated favorites like croissants, pain suisse, and a cinnamon roll, plus macarons and classic patisserie. The menu goes beyond sweets with breakfast items, quiche, baguette sandwiches, and cheese and charcuterie boards, so it works for a quick pastry run or a slower sit-down lunch. Espresso and signature lattes round it out, and the overall vibe is a European bakery stop that feels more like a destination than a convenience pickup, especially if you arrive early before items sell out.
Comments:
Shout out to Alicia S. for finding this spot and being the source of all this good information! This place is so authentically French that some of the staff speak French, one of the owners is French, French conversation hours are hosted here by local language academies, and most people who've visited praise the food and pastries for being legit French (sorry Amelie's, I love you as a place to hang out but there are Frenchier French bakeries around town). 

Another thing to love about this place is the teas. They serve Harney & Sons, which is about as good as it gets when it comes to tea brands used by businesses. The place is co-owned by the couple Alexandre Darbousset and Angela Yeo. The two spent years planning the bakery after moving to the Lake Norman area and noticing there were no authentic French bakeries around. Alexandre brings traditional French baking techniques to the business, and they even source their flour from a French flour mill, Grands Moulins de Paris, to keep things legit.

Also, while it's not a traditional French pastry, they have cinnamon rolls, which I deeply appreciate 🙌🏾🙏🏽. They’re the best cinnamon rolls of any French bakery in the Charlotte area hands down. 10000% better than Amelie’s. Give them a try, at first glance, they look a bit dry, but looks can be misleading, and in this case they are. They’re great!

And speaking of authentic French bakeries in the Charlotte area, another one worth checking out is Mado Bakery and Cafe in Rock Hill. It's owned by a West African couple that lived in France for years before moving stateside. It's also great!

Address: 401 S Tryon St Ste 140, Charlotte, NC 28202
Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description:
One of two Gotcha Matcha & Espresso locations in Uptown Charlotte, this one is inside Three Wells Fargo Center at Suite 140, right by The Green park. The entrance can be tricky to spot from the street since it is inside the office tower's ground-floor retail area rather than a traditional storefront. The shop itself has a few seats inside, and there is a good amount of seating in the hallway outside the storefront within Three Wells Fargo, so finding a place to sit is not usually a problem

Expect the same core menu of matcha lattes, espresso drinks, refresher teas, and pastries, plus some cool additions like Earl Grey matcha, lemon pound cake matcha, Vietnamese iced coffee, Ube cinnamon roll, and the Ube bagel with Ube cream cheese. The space has a brighter, more storefront feel with easier walk-up access compared to the original 200 S Tryon location, and it works well for a daytime coffee run, a casual meet-up near The Green, or a short laptop session. Their other location is about two blocks north at 200 South Tryon.
Comments:
This is their second location in Uptown. It's by The Green inside Three Wells Fargo center, as laid out in this Instagram post. They've got a full coffee shop menu in addition to loose leaf teas and plenty of sweet treats. It can be tricky to find, but look for the signs, and ask anyone around because they can usually point you to it.

Let me get to what's most important though. If there were an award for the most creative cinnamon roll in Charlotte, this spot would win. Magnificent. Extraordinary. Sublime. Transcendent. A revelation. Per this TikTok post, as well as their menu, and my own first-person account, they have what are called "Ube cinnamon rolls." I'm something of a cinnamon roll connoisseur, yet my visit here was my first time hearing of such a thing. So, I asked the staff, and did some research of my own, and discovered that Ube cinnamon rolls are "a Filipino-inspired twist on the classic American cinnamon roll, characterized by its vibrant purple color and a unique earthy, sweet flavor."

I can attest to their brilliance, specifically because of how different these are from a standard cinnamon roll. They are extremely generous with the purple Ube icing, and the roll itself has a sort of brown bread base, maybe even wheat, that comes across as a tad healthier than white bread, maybe to offset the extravagance of the Ube. It's also huge. 

So altogether, you get a massive cinnamon roll with colors that pop and flavors that pop, where the Ube icing is sweet but not the overwhelming buttercream sweet you may be used to in cinnamon rolls. It's just different, in a good way. I really think you should try it, then contact me to let me know what you think. I want to hear from you if the topic is cinnamon rolls.

What a splendid thing this is. For cultures around the world to bring their own unique flavors to the beauty that is a cinnamon roll. This is what we need y'all. Coming together worldwide over ways to bring your own flair to cinnamon rolls. Yes. Cinnamon roll diplomacy. I dig it.

Address: 117 N Pilot Knob Rd, Denver, NC 28037
Hours: ["Monday: 6 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 6 AM - 8 PM", "Saturday: 6 AM - 8 PM", "Sunday: 6 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: Cozy local coffee and boba spot in Denver with a customizable drink menu that covers espresso drinks, milk teas, fruit smoothies, and blended frappes. Often used for casual hangouts, quick work sessions, and small meetups, with a relaxed neighborhood feel and friendly baristas.

Place: Higher Grounds by Manolo's | Eastland Yards
Description: An upcoming specialty coffee shop planned for Eastland Yards, the mixed-use redevelopment of the former Eastland Mall site in East Charlotte. The cafe is expected to bring Colombian coffee, tea, pastries, and light grab-and-go food to an apartment-building retail storefront. The concept is community-focused, with roots in Manolo’s Bakery and the existing Higher Grounds cafe partnership model. Expect a daytime coffee-and-cafe stop with baked goods, espresso drinks, teas, and a mission-driven local-business identity.
Comments: According to this announcement Higher Grounds is expanding to the area that used to be Eastland Mall. Manolo Betancur, owner of Artisen Gelato and Higher Grounds, is excited about the opportunity. “This is a wonderful opportunity where we are showing how the city, private corporations and small businesses can work together and support each other,” he said.  Also mentioned in this article and this one as well.

Hours: ["Monday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Saturday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Sunday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: Artisanal donut and coffee shop with chef-made, small-batch donuts and a full coffee menu in a bright, modern grab-a-box-or-hang-for-a-bit setup.
Comments:
Small spot, but they use the space well. There are 2 small two-person tables, 1 high-top table, a 6-person table in the middle, and 6 bar-style seats along a high table near the window. Still, I’d say it’s better suited for a quick chat than an extended stay.

That said, the pricing for donuts in bulk is wild. $52 for a dozen. I don’t even know how to process that kind of pricing. Inflation is bad, but is it $52 for a dozen donuts bad?

Parking can be tricky. It’s not obvious, but there’s a paid garage behind the building that belongs to an apartment complex. If you’re feeling bold, you can try the nearby CVS Pharmacy lot, though there’s always a risk of getting towed. Oh, and they have cinnamon rolls. They’re solid.

Address: 354 George W Liles Pkwy Suite 60, Concord, NC 28027
Hours: ["Monday: 3 PM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 3 PM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 3 PM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 3 PM - 11 PM", "Friday: 12 PM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 7 PM"]
Description:
Family run tabletop and trading card game shop in Concord built around community play, with a cozy game space where cards clash, stories ignite, and adventures are always a roll away. Expect a curated mix of Magic: The Gathering, Star Wars Unlimited, Dungeons & Dragons, sports cards, and collectibles, plus open tables for casual play, Commander nights, and beginner friendly events.

There is a $5 day pass option that gives access to a growing board game library, and it is also free to browse, shop, or hang out at the counter with no pressure. Snacks and cold drinks are available daily including sweet and salty options, with THC drinks on hand and local beer and wine planned for the future for guests 21+. The vibe is welcoming for first timers and regulars, and staff are happy to help with demos, deck advice, and getting started.

A Gulf-style karak chai café and social lounge in Uptown Charlotte serving authentic karak chai, specialty coffee, handcrafted lattes, cardamom-forward drinks, and desserts with international flavors. The menu centers on chai, espresso drinks, milk cakes, sweet treats, and café items influenced by Gulf, South Asian, Arab, and global dessert traditions. The space is designed as a late-night gathering spot with a multicultural café feel, offering a relaxed place for conversation, studying, casual meetups, dessert, and coffee near Fourth Ward, Uptown offices, sports venues, parks, museums, and entertainment.
Comments: A chai-and-specialty-coffee concept is opening soon in Charlotte with a brand story rooted in Pakistani chai culture, aiming to be a true “third place” for gathering beyond home and work. Expect karak chai as the headline, plus specialty coffee and a dessert-forward menu, with the Charlotte location reported for Uptown’s Fourth Ward near 5th & Poplar.

Description: Haraz continues their expansion into the greater Charlotte area, this time in Indian Land, South Carolina, as evidenced by this Instagram account. Opening soon at CrossRidge in Indian Land.

Address: 2373 Len Patterson Rd Ste 103, Fort Mill, SC 29708
Hours: ["Monday: 6:30 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 6:30 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 6:30 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 6:30 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 6:30 AM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 6:30 AM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 6:30 AM - 10 PM"]
Description:
A Yemeni-style coffee shop in a Fort Mill shopping center serving traditional coffee drinks, espresso drinks, spiced teas, refreshers, matcha drinks, pastries, and cakes. The space is meant for lingering rather than only grab-and-go, with a polished cafe atmosphere suited for casual meetups, solo coffee stops, dessert runs, and relaxed conversation.

The menu leans more distinctive than a standard coffee shop, with drinks built around cardamom, saffron, honey, cream, coffee husks, ginger, cinnamon, and house spice blends. It is a practical non-alcoholic hangout option for the Fort Mill and Tega Cay side of the Charlotte metro.
Comments:
Of all the now many Haraz locations in the greater Charlotte area, none provides such great access to outlets as this one. It's marvelous. Just about every single one of the 20+ seats in the place give you access to an outlet. It's heavenly for a remote worker like me, or for students, or for anyone looking for a place to hang out on their laptop. Marvelously designed.

Aside from that, if you've been to any Haraz location, you've been to all of them, and I mean that in a good way. Clean, pristine, refined aesthetic, golden accents everywhere, spotless floors, Yemeni artwork and decals on the walls, lots of good cheesecakes and Middle Eastern coffee flavors. They might be a chain, but they're a good one.

This location specifically is just one long hallway with two person tables the whole way, then at each end some larger tables for groups. That design means most seating locations face you towards the entire store, so if you're looking for a quiet place to read a book, this isn't it. Unless you bring noise cancelling headphones (I highly recommend the Sony XM series, I have the XM4's, one of the best purchases I've ever made, I can't hear a thing around me with them on).

Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 12 AM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 12 AM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 9 PM"]
Description: Modern brewery and restaurant in Birkdale Village that doubles as a coffee bar in the mornings and a lively taproom and kitchen later in the day. Inside you will find high ceilings, a long bar, communal tables, and standard dining tables, with additional seating on a patio that faces the main village walkways. The menu runs from espresso drinks and breakfast items in the earlier hours to burgers, bowls, and shareable plates alongside house beers and cocktails at lunch and dinner. With free village Wi-Fi and plenty of seating, it can work for casual laptop time during slower parts of the day, though the space gets noisy and more social during peak meal and evening hours.

Address: 8320 University Executive Park Dr B, Charlotte, NC 28262
Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 11 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 11 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 11 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 11 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 1 AM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 1 AM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 11 PM"]
Description: Yemeni style coffee shop in University City with a big, modern interior, soft lighting, and lots of seating for singles, pairs, and small groups, plus some outdoor tables when the weather is good. Expect a long menu of espresso drinks, traditional Yemeni coffee and tea, and a pastry case loaded with saffron milk cakes, cheesecakes, and savory pastries rather than simple drip and muffins. The shop keeps long hours, draws a mix of students and remote workers during the day, and stays lively into the evening as a social hangout for people who want a non-alcoholic spot to meet up, study, or talk over dessert and coffee.
Comments:
Haraz is now undoubtedly a staple in the Charlotte area, with this being their fourth location after Plaza Midwood, LoSo, and Ballantyne (with even more locations on the way). And like all their other spots, it’s gorgeous. Polished faux marble tables, black and gold accents everywhere, every corner of the place pristine and clean. Gives it a very calming vibe, with this spot specifically having large floor to ceiling windows letting in a lot of natural light.

There’s plenty of seating, with my favorite spots being the two person tables back towards the far wall. Feels out of the way and snug. If you’ve been to the Plaza Midwood location and struggled to find seating, that’ll be less of a problem here. They even have a living room looking lounge area with a couch and glass doors that I believe you can book if you’re bringing a group. 

Parking right next to the store is in a garage, but the general area has a hotel and other businesses with lots you can park in if it’s full. Access to outlets is solid but not great, the booth style wrap around seating thing in the middle has no outlets, which is a bummer because those are great seats. But there are some along the back wall and near the entrance seating.

But let’s get to the most important thing here. This is the only Haraz location (that I know of) that has cinnamon rolls. They're medium sized, with cream cheese frosting that tastes almost exactly like the kind used by Amelie's, and pair nicely with a hot tea or chocolate. I’m glad I came here rather than assuming it’d be like all the other Haraz locations. I mean, it is in terms of decor, but that’s a good thing because all their locations are gorgeous. But having cinnamon rolls? This is now my favorite of all of them. I’m devoutly Christian, but they had me singing Alhamdulillah 🙌🏾🙏🏾 (which is the Arabic equivalent of Hallelujah and means praise be to God or thank God). Cinnamon rolls make any location better!

Description:
Small Christian coffee shop on South Tryon rooted in Biblical principles and focused on more than just serving coffee. The owners describe it as a kind of sacred space where coffee is a bridge for connection, conversation and hospitality that reflects Christ’s love. 

You will find handcrafted espresso drinks, frappes, teas and a rotating selection of baked goods, along with gospel focused open mics, mixers and other community events that blend culture, community and faith. The atmosphere is intentionally warm and ministry minded rather than corporate, with an emphasis on people leaving refreshed, encouraged and more grounded in their sense of purpose.
Comments:
The very definition of a hidden gem. I found out about it by word of mouth (shout out to Istar!). Owned by Eritrean locals who’ve filled the space with both Habesha culture and nods to their Christian values, which I love as a Christian myself. It’s one of those spots that feels genuine the moment you walk in.

There’s a bit of outdoor seating and more inside through the back room. When you first step in, you’ll see a couch and a few high-top tables, but head to the back and you’ll find more spots to sit, including a big table that’s great for groups. It’s compact but cozy.

Free Wi-Fi, great coffee, and a warm, friendly staff make it easy to stay awhile. They also host music nights, including gospel saxophone performances and other Christian-themed events that give the place real community energy. A true third place in every sense.

Address: 330 Camp Rd Suite B, Charlotte, NC 28206
Hours: ["Monday: 12 PM - 7 PM", "Tuesday: 12 PM - 7 PM", "Wednesday: 12 PM - 7 PM", "Thursday: 12 PM - 7 PM", "Friday: 12 PM - 8 PM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 7 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 6 PM"]
Description: Used bookstore at Camp North End with bright floors, tall shelves of gently loved paperbacks and hardcovers, and a small selection of new titles from local authors. The space is connected to a coworking hub and set up as a gathering spot, with cozy seating, spots to read or work on a laptop, and coffee available. Regular author events, book clubs, and community gatherings make it an easy place to drop in, browse for a while, and then wander out into the rest of Camp North End’s food stalls, art, and outdoor seating.
Comments:
A friend of mine mentioned she attends a Silent Book Club here. I'd never heard of that concept, and now that I have, I love it. You meet up with a group of people, you all bring a book, you read in silence for a set amount of time, and after you've all read, you then socialize. It's a beautiful concept and now I want to try it. Our attention spans are lower than ever with digital devices pulling us away from reading all the time. Having a community to just sit and read with, that's just wonderful man.

This spot is in Camp North End, a third place in and of itself, which makes the whole area worth visiting. On warm days, the seating area right outside the shop is great. It's in a sort of enclave section that makes it feel snug. And back behind the building there's a great spot with those swing bench things that's perfect for reading in seclusion. When they host events, they expand into nearby Hygge Coworking, so while the shop is small, they end up with plenty of space to host large groups.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 9 PM"]
Description:
Casual spot at Camp North End featuring indoor and outdoor pickleball courts, golf simulators, a bar and Tex Mex food, all set up for groups to play or just hang out. There's a wide variety of seating from sofas to tables, with free Wi-Fi and plenty of free parking around the campus.

You order by scanning a QR code on your phone, so staff only check in every now and then, which makes it easy to settle in for a while without worrying about table turnover. The space is lively with steady background noise rather than quiet study vibes, and the food is cheap and tasty enough to carry you through a long session of games and people watching.
Comments:
Fun fact, I got to tour this spot before it opened while helping plan a pickleball tournament for a nonprofit I volunteer with, the Carolina Youth Coalition. They support high-achieving, under-resourced students on their journey to and through college. If you’re looking for ways to get involved, you should absolutely volunteer with them!

As for Tipsy Pickle, I got to see this place transform from an unfinished shell into one of the hottest pickleball and social spots in town. Our tournament went great, largely thanks to the Tipsy Pickle team who handled everything the day of, from food to drinks to logistics. We just focused on the prep work, and they made the event a success.

It’s located in Camp North End, which means free parking in the big gravel lot toward the back and plenty of other things to do nearby. Great location and an even better atmosphere. The food and drinks are solid, the vibe is lively, and it’s a great place not just to play pickleball but to grab a bite, sit at the bar, and hang out. There are two levels, plus plenty of outdoor space, so if you're looking for a place to meet a group of friends, this is a great pick.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 10 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: Informal, contemporary hangout with indoor and outdoor pickleball courts, craft cocktails and gourmet coffee.
Comments:
Plenty of space here. Definitely not the spot if you’re looking for peace and quiet, but top tier if you want a lively, energetic, and social crowd. You don’t have to come for pickleball either. It’s also a restaurant, bar, and café serving coffee, pastries, lattes, smoothies, and tea. Whatever you’re in the mood for, they’ve got it.

If you’re planning to hang out and work on your laptop, bring noise-canceling headphones (the Sony XM4s are still the all-time champs). There’s always music playing and the sound of pickleball paddles in the background, but it’s still a solid work spot if you don’t mind a little noise. Mornings are much calmer, and there’s a ton of lounge-style seating, comfy couches, and tables. If it’s warm, there’s also tons of outdoor seating.

The food is solid, parking is free, and the staff are really friendly. It’s clear they’re intentional about making people feel welcome and giving the space a laid-back, friendly vibe. Everyone I’ve talked to has been easygoing and helpful. Good vibes all around.

Place: To The Table Board Game Cafe
Hours: ["Monday: 6 PM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 6 PM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 6 PM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 6 PM - 10 PM", "Friday: 6 PM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 12 PM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 9 PM"]
Description: Cozy board game cafe in Matthews offering a rental library of modern board games, trading card games, and role-playing games alongside drinks and light snacks. Guests pay a modest table or game fee, pick a title from the shelves, and settle in at cafe style seating or themed rooms for a few hours of play with friends or organized events like Commander and D&D nights. Located in a neighborhood shopping center with on site parking and free Wi-Fi, it caters to both serious gamers and casual groups looking for a laid back spot to play, meet new people, and try new games.

Address: 6908 Matthews-mint Hill Rd #350, Mint Hill, NC 28227
Hours: ["Monday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Friday: 12 PM - 12 AM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Family friendly tabletop shop in Mint Hill with walls of board games, card games, and RPG supplies plus plenty of in store tables for open play. A large game library lets you try new titles in the space or rent them to take home, and there are private rooms and regular events for RPGs, trading card games, and miniatures. Located in a suburban shopping center with easy lot parking, it functions as a community hub where locals drop in to browse, learn new games, and spend hours playing with friends.

Hours: ["Monday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Friday: 12 PM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 8 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 6 PM"]
Description:
Downtown Concord game and hobby shop located in a historic former bank building, offering a wide selection of board games, trading card games, RPGs, puzzles, and miniatures. The main floor doubles as a retail space and play area, with multiple tables set up for casual gaming, tournaments, puzzle competitions, and regular events for games like Magic, Pokémon, and Warhammer.

The staff are known for being welcoming and knowledgeable, helping new and experienced players find the right games or cards. Snacks, soft drinks, and treats are available, free Wi-Fi makes it easy to look up rules or deck lists, and customers typically spend several hours here during leagues and events. Parking is via nearby public decks and downtown street parking.

Type: Comic Book Store,Game Store,Ice Cream Shop
Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Friday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 12 PM - 8 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 7 PM"]
Description:
Neighborhood comic and game shop in Northwest Charlotte that combines new comics, graphic novels, and trading card products with a family friendly play space. The current location has an expanded seating and group play area, plus a connected ice cream counter offering shakes, floats, and a big selection of cold drinks.

Regular events for Magic the Gathering, Pokémon, Dungeons & Dragons, and other games draw a consistent community of players of all ages. Private lot parking is available on site, and the vibe is more hang out and play games than sit quietly and work on a laptop.

Address: 1640 Sardis Rd N Ste 110, Charlotte, NC 28270
Hours: ["Monday: 5 PM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: Closed", "Wednesday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Friday: 12 PM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 9 AM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 9 PM"]
Description:
Board game cafe and wine bar in southeast Charlotte with a library of modern and classic tabletop games, a full coffee and tea program that includes a reservation only coffee omakase at the bar, and a menu of sandwiches, waffles, gelato, and small sweets.

Guests can reserve time or walk in to play from a curated list of family friendly and strategy games while enjoying espresso drinks, teas, beer, cider, and wine by the glass or bottle. The space is set up with a mix of lounge seating and game tables suited for couples, families, and small groups, and offers free Wi-Fi for light laptop use between turns.
Comments:
Very comfortable aesthetic. Dimly lit, well spaced out, not cramped, smells good, usually some soft jazzy music playing in the background, understated lamps on tables, and a bar that serves alcohol but also everything. Food, coffee, loose leaf teas (the Milk Oolong and Peach Paradise are 🔥), cold drinks, whatever you want they got it. Incredibly well done, whoever designed this space.

It’s a board game cafe, so that’s the highlight of the place. You can drop in with no appointment and just grab some food or drink, or you can grab a game table with friends and play one of the many board games they provide. While they have Wi-Fi, I wouldn’t come here to work remotely or study. I mean you could, but it doesn’t fit the vibe. Instead play some games, drink some great tea, or read a book as I’ve done a few times here. I fully support having more of these places in Charlotte!

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 11 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 11 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 11 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 11 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 1 AM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 1 AM", "Sunday: 9 AM - 10 PM"]
Description: Outpost of a local mini-chain for organic produce, wine, craft beer & gourmet meats & cheeses.
Comments: Has some solid outdoor seating on the rail trail, great for people watching, or watching the light rail go by. They've gone viral a few times, at least within Charlotte, for their hot sandwiches. Small interior but there are tables here and there and free Wi-Fi (ask staff for the details, and a hint, it's a capital H and capital G in the password). I'd say this space is best suited for using their patio to meet friends and chill. If you need more space, it’s a short walk from Night Swim.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 6 PM"]
Description: Specialty coffee shop from a local Charlotte roaster serving carefully crafted espresso drinks, drip coffee, and rotating single origin offerings, along with a small selection of pastries. Known for consistent quality, attention to detail, and baristas who can help you choose something new, it is a reliable stop whether you are grabbing a quick coffee or exploring more adventurous seasonal drinks.
Comments: Super small, most of the seating is coffee bar style with high chairs, so maybe not a place if your goal is to stay a while.

Address: 1101 Central Ave Unit B, Charlotte, NC 28204
Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: Closed", "Wednesday: 6 PM - 11 PM", "Thursday: 6 PM - 11 PM", "Friday: 6 PM - 12 AM", "Saturday: 6 PM - 12 AM", "Sunday: 6 PM - 11 PM"]
Description: Intimate vinyl listening bar tucked inside the lobby of a boutique hotel on Central Avenue, with a cozy lounge, projector visuals, and a high-quality sound system focused on curated DJ sets and deep cut playlists. Expect craft cocktails, wine, beer, and a few spirits free options, but no kitchen, so you'll need to bring or order in your own food.
Comments:
Found by a friend of mine, Alex Jones (not the controversial right-wing figure, this Alex is a black woman, just an unfortunate coincidence with the name, she's way cooler than the other Alex Jones). 

This is in a unique hotel called Hotel Refuge, worth checking out, they have an interesting story. The space itself for the bar is small with limited seating that suits dates or groups of four or fewer, and it can get busy on weekends, so reservations are recommended. I'd say it works best as an evening hangout spot for music lovers rather than a full-on work or study space.

Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 9 PM"]
Description: 21+ Amsterdam style cannabis lounge and coffee bar in South End with a cozy indoor lounge and a turf patio set up for social smoking. Expect regular and infused coffee drinks, zero proof mocktails, and a rotating pastry case, plus occasional infused treats. The space doubles as a hangout spot with board games, weekly trivia, DJ nights, and four big TVs, and it is set up for laptop work with tables and power during quieter hours.
Comments: If cannabis is your thing, you'll love this place.

Hours: ["Monday: 11:30 AM - 8:30 PM", "Tuesday: 11:30 AM - 8:30 PM", "Wednesday: 11:30 AM - 8:30 PM", "Thursday: 11:30 AM - 8:30 PM", "Friday: 11:30 AM - 9:30 PM", "Saturday: 11:30 AM - 9:30 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 8 PM"]
Description: Spacious bubble tea shop on South Blvd with plenty of room to sit and hang out, serving a large menu of milk teas, fruit teas, slushes, and toppings, plus free Wi-Fi and on-site lot parking.
Comments: They have really good hot chocolate. Not too sweet, just right. There's an overwhelming amount of tea options, many of which you can get hot. There's a solid amount of space for hanging out. 4 4-top tables, then 4 more 2-top tables, then some bar style highchair seating and a couch plus some lounge chairs. Not at all cramped, there's room to spread out. And it doesn't seem to get super busy here, so it's a solid place to plop down to work remote, read, study, or meet some friends.

Address: 9715 Sam Furr Rd D, Huntersville, NC 28078
Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 9 PM"]
Description: Compact bubble tea shop in Northcross Shopping Center offering customizable milk and fruit teas, Korean fried chicken, Korean hot dogs, egg waffles, and desserts, with a decent number of tables for casual hangouts or study sessions.

Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 10 PM"]
Description: Modern bubble tea and snack shop in Plaza Midwood serving milk teas, fruit teas, fried chicken, and Hong Kong–style egg waffles inside a sit-down cafe space with shared parking off Central Ave.
Comments:
This is right next to the original Haraz location in Plaza Midwood and shares the same parking lot. Which means, parking is rough if you go at any reasonable time. It’s free but the lot gets packed on account of being shared with multiple businesses.

That aside, this place has an aesthetic that seems consistent across bubble tea stores in general. Bright colors, toy vending machines, a good amount of seating, and free Wi-Fi. This is a great alternative to Haraz if you’re in the area and looking for a place to plop down and work or study. There’s solid access to outlets and enough seats to feel comfortable about finding a spot.

Food and drink wise, they have very expensive chicken options, and an egg waffle that’s their specialty. And of course, plenty of bubble teas. What’s interesting here is you’re sort of expected to order on a tablet. There’s two big ones when you first walk in and more at the back of the store. You can order at the counter but it’s pretty clear they’d prefer you use the tablets. Which is…very modern.

As an introvert, I actually quite like it, I just wish you didn’t have to sign up for an account to complete your order. I have a Google Voice number I use for those situations, but I suspect most people are out here handing out their personal cellphone numbers, which I don’t like. Why do you need my phone number to facilitate an age old transaction of ordering food or drink in person? Like, I’m at the store. Maybe I’m just being unreasonable idk. I’m a data privacy nerd.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: Coffee bar tucked inside Compare Foods, a Latin supermarket on South Blvd, serving espresso drinks, flavored lattes, and Latin-inspired treats from a small counter near the front of the store.
Comments:
This is the definition of a hidden gem. You're unlikely to know it exists unless it's mentioned to you, given that it's inside Compare Foods, and even the sign for it isn't terribly visible from the road. As you walk in the store, it's to your right.

The coffee shop has, in addition to all standard coffee options, ice cream, smoothies, pastries, and some food items. But for food, you also have a couple of restaurants inside the store, each of which are great, so you won't go hungry. Seating is in a cafe area that's shared between the coffee shop and the restaurants. Not a ton of space, or outlets, but there's enough to say you're likely to find somewhere to sit if it's not too busy.

This is a great place to work remotely from on a random weekday, as long as you bring noise cancelling headphones. It's usually not that busy, but they do play music throughout the store, so it's by no means quiet. Oh, and if you want to practice your Spanish, this is the place to be. Just about anyone you talk to here will speak Spanish!

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: 5 PM - 12 AM", "Wednesday: 5 PM - 12 AM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 2 AM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 2 AM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 12 AM"]
Description: Coffeehouse/full bar offering live music & comedy nightly in a relaxed setting with outdoor seating.
Comments:
There’s a cute border collie that hangs out here sometimes. The space is one long hallway with an unfinished floor, a stage area with seating for live music shows, and then a cozy corner with armchairs, a sofa, and a couch.

They’ve got coffee, tea, and all sorts of alcoholic options. The aesthetic is punk rock and alternative, with stickers all over the place, artwork on the walls, graffiti on some of the couches, and a general counterculture “got a tattoo to piss off my conservative parents” sort of vibe. The parking lot is small, but drive around and you might be able to find street parking nearby.

Hours: ["Monday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Tuesday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Wednesday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Thursday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Friday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: The Photo Outfitters is a community focused camera shop and film lab offering new and used gear, film developing, darkroom and studio rentals, and photography classes for all levels. It doubles as a hangout spot for local photographers, with a living room style seating area where people can meet, talk shop, and plan their next shoot.
Comments:
If you're into photography, this is your third place. The owners quite literally used the words "third place" when discussing what they want the space to be on this podcast. The heart of the space is the lounge area, with a deep blue couch, coffee table, walls full of framed prints, and a big world map dotted with pins where photos were taken. 

You're welcome to sit, ask questions, and stay a while without buying a thing. They host meetups, classes, gallery nights, and gear events, so the space is constantly rotating between shop, classroom, and clubhouse for Charlotte’s photography crowd.

If you want something to drink, grab a coffee at Night Swim | Oakhurst right around the corner, then wander over here to hang out and talk all manner of photography nerd stuff to your hearts content.

Hours: ["Monday: 6 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 9 PM"]
Description: Counter-serve bakery/cafe chain serving sandwiches, salads & more, known for its bread & free WiFi.
Comments:
This is my favorite Panera in the greater Charlotte area. It's huge, not only the main seating area, but they have a conference room that's sometimes not booked, so you can go in there and sit as well. Between the inside and outside seating, it'd be impossible to come here and not find a place to sit. Other than that, it's a Panera Bread. You know what it is.

There's a seating booth here that's to your right as you walk in that's an excellent spot to plop down. It's back aganist a wall, and when you're seated there's a half wall with a window into the bakery area that blocks your view of the rest of the space. So, most visual distractions are gone. I'm typing this comment from there right now. If you're reading this comment and ever come here, try to snag that spot. But if I also happen to be coming in and see you there, we'll have to fight for it 🥊😂. Jokes aside, it's a prime spot to lock in, study, work remotely, get some reading done, have a private conversation, whatever it is you do.

Address: 7714 Matthews-mint Hill Rd, Mint Hill, NC 28227
Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: Closed", "Wednesday: 5:30 PM - 8:30 PM", "Thursday: 5 PM - 8:30 PM", "Friday: 5:30 PM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 11:30 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 5 PM"]
Description: A black woman owned cultural gallery, tea lounge, and bookstore, all in one intimate space. It features rotating Black art, The Ebony Shelf’s curated selection of books by Black and minority authors, and the Ebony Bloom Tea Lounge for tea service, treats, and community events in historic Mint Hill.
Comments:
This spot is a whole ecosystem of Black culture tucked into a single Mint Hill storefront. Inside, you have The Ebony Shelf, a bookstore where the shelves are heavy on Black and minority authors, the kind of titles that never get the center table at the big chains. There are children’s books, suspense, memoirs, and all sorts of random gems.

Inside that same space you have Ebony Bloom Tea Lounge, a full tea and coffee room in that old school afternoon tea style that's faded but is making a comeback (see The Pauline Tea-Bar Apothecary and A Cause for Tea). They have bistro tables, floral touches, teacups, and various photos of Black cultural icons. The owner is very intentional about this being a welcoming place, especially for the Black community, encouraging you to dress up a bit if you want and make it a spot of your own. Given that I, the creator of this site, am black, I love it!

Then there's the art gallery side, which is basically an open invitation for local artists to take over, hang their work, and host whatever gathering fits the moment. It's one building boasting a bookstore, tea lounge, art gallery, and community room all weaved together and inspired by Black culture.

It's a third place true and through. You can browse a new-to-you Black author, sip tea, and then stick around for an art opening or a themed night. Check out their Google Maps profile and Instagram for exact hours of operation. It's worth a visit!

Place: The Giddy Goat Coffee Roasters | South End
Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 2 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 2 PM"]
Description: Spacious and stylish coffee shop in South End with a bright, modern interior and a laid-back community feel. Known for its excellent coffee, fresh empanadas, and friendly staff.
Comments:
This is their newest location in South End, with the original spot still going strong in Plaza Midwood. This one completely lives up to the hype. Honestly, I’d go so far as to say it’s the best place to hang out in South End if you’re a remote worker, a student, or just someone looking for a spot to plop down with your laptop.

Access to outlets is tremendous. Truly 10/10. You can tell there was an intentional effort to make it easy to stay charged, and I love whoever made that decision. There’s plenty of seating both inside and out, and the outdoor setup is quality. There’s a ground-level patio and an upstairs patio, and both have outlets. Outdoors! They even have the little plastic covers to keep them protected from the elements, so you can sit in the sun, plug in, and just relax.

They’ve got pastries (no cinnamon rolls, unfortunately) but some solid options, plus empanadas and other grab-and-go bites. It’s already a popular remote work spot. Every time I’ve been, there’s a steady group of people hanging out, working, and enjoying the space. The design is excellent, with plenty of room to spread out, good energy, and great coffee. I’m more of a tea person, but they’ve got that too, with some solid choices.

The only real concern is parking. There’s free street parking, but this is South End, so it’s limited, and apartments nearby make it competitive. There are some paid lots further down the street if needed.

The upstairs patio is also a great place to read on a warm day, with solid views and a calm atmosphere. Overall, this is an incredible addition to the South End coffee shop scene. I’d choose this place over nearby Not Just Coffee, Stable Hand, Salted Melon, and even Suffolk Punch, which has long been one of my favorites in the area. It’s that good.

Hours: ["Monday: 5 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 5 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 5 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 5 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 5 AM - 8 PM", "Saturday: 5 AM - 8 PM", "Sunday: 5 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: Seattle-based coffeehouse chain known for its signature roasts, light bites and WiFi availability.
Comments:
There are book clubs that meet here and love the space. There's plenty of seating, and its top tier as far as Starbucks' go in terms of interior design. Access to outlets is excellent. There's a comfy booth style seating area, and some padded lounge chairs by the windows that are great for feeling nestled in. Then there's a bunch of individual seating tables, and a very cool mural created by @napoletanoart that makes it feel less corporate coffee shop and more local spot.

Parking isn't great, but it isn't terrible as far as South End goes. There's a free lot, but it's shared with other businesses, so at peak times it can get packed. But at least there's a free parking lot. That's a win just in and of itself. Not all Starbucks are created equal. This is one of the better ones.

Address: 15205 John J Delaney Dr Ste B, Charlotte, NC 28277
Hours: ["Monday: 6:30 AM - 11 PM", "Tuesday: 6:30 AM - 11 PM", "Wednesday: 6:30 AM - 11 PM", "Thursday: 6:30 AM - 11 PM", "Friday: 6:30 AM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 6:30 AM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 6:30 AM - 11 PM"]
Description:
The country of Yemen is unapparelled to any other place on this Earth. From its breathtaking scenery to its history that dates back to the beginning of man. Yemen is the homeland of our family. As first-generation immigrants, we wanted to honor our heritage.

Yemen is where coffee was invented and later flourished in global trade. Roasting and serving coffee is something that is inclusive of all people and cultures and brings people together. Our coffee is grown from altitudes over 3,000 ft in a mountainous village called Haraz, where our namesake originates.
Comments:
As beautiful as all their other locations. This one is now my favorite compared to their Elizabeth and LoSo spots since parking is super easy (it's in a shopping complex with a huge lot), and access to outlets is truly marvelous. 10/10. There was an intentional effort here to make sure people coming to work on laptops or study could stay charged up and I appreciate it.

Aside from that, if you've been to any of their other locations and enjoyed it, this is worth a visit. They're open late as well, so its a great spot to meet friends, study, work, do whatever you need to, late into the evening. And Ballantyne is one of the nicest suburbs of Charlotte. Check out Ballantyne's Backyard and the The Bowl at Ballantyne (right across the street) while you're here. There's a whole project called Ballantyne Reimagined that focuses on adding more and more cool stuff to the area. They've already done a great job!

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: 6:30 AM - 3 PM", "Wednesday: 6:30 AM - 3 PM", "Thursday: 6:30 AM - 3 PM", "Friday: 6:30 AM - 3 PM", "Saturday: 6:30 AM - 3 PM", "Sunday: 6:30 AM - 3 PM"]
Description: Laid-back shop specializing in yeast-raised donuts, some with specialty fillings, plus coffee.
Comments:
Small, bright neighborhood shop with donuts, coffee, tea, cinnamon rolls (that I might argue are closer to a coffee roll, but I'll let it slide) and cute vibes. There's a handful of tables plus a cozy couch, and a couple of outdoor spots if you want to sit in the sun and eat frosting. Do not come here if you're trying to eat healthier. You'll fail.

This is very much a morning third place, the kind of spot where you meet a friend, read a book, catch up on some news, watch a few YouTube videos, think about your life and whether you're making sufficient progress toward the goals you set at the beginning of the year, reflect on the fact that by being in this shop eating a donut you're probably failing at at least one of them, and then walk out sugared up and happy.

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: 6:30 AM - 3 PM", "Wednesday: 6:30 AM - 3 PM", "Thursday: 6:30 AM - 3 PM", "Friday: 6:30 AM - 3 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 3 PM"]
Description: Bakery specializing in donuts such as apple fritters and buttermilk varieties, plus Vietnamese coffee.
Comments:
Owned by the same people as OMG Donuts & Bakery in Concord. It's a small space, but it still manages to fit a decent amount of seating so you can sit and hang out for a while. The business clearly encourages that since they provide fast and free Wi-Fi. The donuts, coffee, and ice cream are all really good. So if you’re on a diet, don’t come here. Everything is sweet, and everything is good.

They also sell cinnamon rolls, which, as a connoisseur in that field, I’d argue are more accurately described as coffee rolls, but I’ll let it slide. Mostly because on my first visit, a food truck called Cookin & Cruzin was parked outside, selling marvelous breakfast sandwiches and cinnamon rolls from Beyond Amazing Donuts. 

Beyond Amazing has long been on my list of best cinnamon rolls in Charlotte, currently surpassed only by Honeybear Bake Shop and tied for second with Sunflour Bakery. They’re thick, sweet, perfectly glazed, and powerfully pleasing cinnamon rolls. If you haven’t had one, it’s worth the trip to their location, formerly on Park Road and now in Uptown.

I’m not sure if Cookin & Cruzin is always posted up outside this place, but if they are, that’s a good and godly thing. OMG Donuts is a great spot to hang out, especially on a Sunday afternoon. If the food truck is around, you can grab a donut, a coffee, then hangout a bit, grab a breakfast sandwich, and a thick cinnamon roll all in one go. Praise be.

Hours: ["Monday: 5 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 5 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 5 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 5 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 5 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 5 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 5 AM - 9 PM"]
Description: Seattle-based coffeehouse chain known for its signature roasts, light bites and WiFi availability.
Comments:
If you're like "oh man Starbucks is boring, why is it on this website, what about the non-chain coffee shops", calm down alright. There's a lot of those on this website. I found myself here out of desperation, the nearby Night Swim closes at 5 PM, and I had a meeting right at that time I needed to be at a laptop for. This was the one of the closest results when I typed in coffee on Google Maps, and it worked quite well.

I would never tell you to go out of your way to come here. It's a cookie cutter Starbucks. But they have good access to outlets, and a decent amount of seating. They have booth style seating with around 8 small tables with outlets near each one. Then 5 or so tables by the window, no outlet access. Then the big table in the middle with outlets underneath it, and some lounge chair style seating. So, marvelous outlet access all around.

On my first (and only) visit here, a family came in and sat in the lounge seating, catching up, chatting, and petting their comically small, but very cute, dog. This place certainly is a third place for them, so it is for me. It's Starbucks, you know what it is. But it works. If you're in this area and want something truly local and nonchain, check out Visart Cafe and Julia's Cafe and Books.

A final note, I don't enjoy Independence Blvd. Having what is essentially a 5-lane highway right next to retail shops with people entering and exiting at wildly different speeds is some sort of masochist masterclass in road design. I came here out of desperation. I will only return under similar circumstances. There is no God-fearing reason to voluntarily sign up to drive on Independence Blvd.

Hours: ["Monday: 7:30 AM - 2 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 4 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 4 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 4 PM", "Friday: 6 AM - 4 PM", "Saturday: 6 AM - 2 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Small, scratch-made neighborhood bakery with early-morning breakfast sandwiches on house bread plus a rotating case of pastries and custom-order cakes. Expect a quick counter-service setup and the best selection earlier in the day. Parking is straightforward and free.
Comments:
They have marvelous, terrific, glorious, splendid, sizeable, positively charming cinnamon rolls. I first came here for that reason alone, and only after walking in discovered it's also a third place. There are a few seating spots, and they serve food as well. 

It's small, really small, but worth visiting, for the cinnamon rolls, and the chance that it's not too busy and you can snag a spot to sit down. Maybe best for meeting friends or family for a chat, rather than working remote or studying. Lovely none the less. Down to earth, local, and they have cinnamon rolls 🙌🏾.

Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 6 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 6 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 6 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 7 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 7 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 4 PM"]
Description: A specialty coffee shop inside The District Exchange in downtown Concord serving espresso drinks, pour-overs, rotating single-origin coffees, seasonal drinks, local bagels, artisan pastries, and locally roasted coffee beans. The space has a bright, relaxed lounge feel with leather couches, exposed brick, vintage bookshelves, retail items, and room for browsing, working, meeting, or sitting with coffee. The shop emphasizes ethically sourced coffee, compostable cups, zero single-use plastic, and a sustainability-minded approach to café service. Good for morning coffee, casual meetups, laptop work, pour-over coffee, pastries, browsing small businesses, and spending time in a historic downtown Concord marketplace.
Comments:
This is inside of a larger space called The District Exchange. They have their own corner though, and while small, it’s cool. There’s a bookcase, a case of vinyl records, and a large table for groups plus some smaller spots off to the side. So not a ton of seating, but there’s some more in the larger building that’s shared between all the shops. Outlet access, both here and in the entire space, is challenging, so charge your laptop if you're coming to work or study.

This whole area is nice though. Downtown Concord is like a Hallmark movie town. Family owned and local store fronts, bookstores, coffee shops, restaurants, a library, and all of it very walkable. There are a ton of third places in the area to hang out at, just check the map. If you’ve never been, it’s worth a trip. And if you come here specifically, there’s a bakery down the street called The Sweet Life with great cinnamon rolls.

Description:
Inspired by shopping arcades from around the world, the District Exchange’s vision is to showcase 22 Union as it was originally intended, as a thriving mixed-use epicenter for historic downtown Concord. Connect, create, shop, and dine across two expansive levels, including a reimagined mezzanine opening to the public for the first time since 1977. 

The District Exchange begins renovations early 2025 to become downtown Concord’s only multi-level retail and dining destination, featuring an inspired mix of staffed merchants and experiences—all under one roof.
Comments:
Large space with a bunch of shops inside, including Crane Coffee, which is easily the most welcoming spot in the building. There’s Wi-Fi, but it’s specific to each business, so your best bet is to grab something from Crane Coffee and ask for their password. 

Upstairs, there are some rocking chairs where you can sit and benevolently peer down at everyone below as they scuttle about fueling the American economy. Which is to say, they're excellent people watching seats. Aside from that, there's a few large tables, a few small tables, and then the seating within Crane Coffee. Overall, compared to the size of the building, there isn't a ton of seating, but what they have is sufficient.

Now, I wouldn’t call this place cozy, and it’s definitely not the spot to read quietly, study, or work remotely. It’s better for meeting friends, walking around, and chatting for a while. The whole Downtown Concord area has plenty of great third places, so if you haven’t explored it yet, it’s worth a visit. Just check the map!

Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Friday: 9 AM - 2:30 PM", "Saturday: Closed", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Coffee shop located in the beautiful Refinery building with a full host of food/beverage offerings (including of course, our home roasted coffee). See you soon!
Comments:
If you’re familiar with their Uptown location, it’s closed. Indigo Tea & Coffee Co. has taken over their old location. This is their new spot inside a larger building called The Refinery. The coffee shop is just a window inside. The seating is from the building itself, and there's not much of it. There are some tall tables with tall chairs, coffee bar style, and then two coffee table, couch, and lounge chair combo areas. 

One of them is half outdoors, which is actually pretty cool. It's at the far end of the hallway behind a glass door. There's a glass wall, two solid brick walls, and then the fourth "wall" is a half open window with plants adorning the sill. Top tier place to sit on a warm day and read a book, there's even outlets in that section, and the Wi-Fi reaches out there.

They have orange flavored cinnamon rolls 🔥🙌🏾 and serve properly steeped loose-leaf tea (no bag in your cup, just the brewed tea). Now, parking is in a paid garage, but they'll validate your ticket, so bring it in with you.

Aside from that, I'd say unless you really like what they're serving, this isn't a place to drive across town to work remote, study, or hangout at. The parking and the small space make it more of a convenience spot, if you're in the area, check it out for sure.

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: Closed", "Wednesday: Closed", "Thursday: 12 PM - 6 PM", "Friday: 9 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 9 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 6 PM"]
Description:
The Book Lady of Monroe specializes in used books. Walking into the store is a bit like stepping into Narnia’s Wardrobe or Dr. Who’s Tardis, there is so much more than is apparent from the outside. You can enjoy settling in one of the several cozy sitting corners to visit or read a while. Carol Gordon, The Book Lady, has made sure to fill every nook and cranny of her 3,900 square feet with a fantastic selection. 

From our exceptional children’s section and every genre of fiction and nonfiction including Christian, history, biography, art, science, home, gardening, health, crafts, cookbooks and more, The Book Lady of Monroe has it all. So stop in and browse, take a peek around the corner.
Comments:
Huge selection and really well organized, which is rare for a used bookstore. It’s so easy to find what you’re looking for here, and the prices are super fair. You can even trade in books if you want. If you’re a bookworm (✋🏽), you could get lost here for hours browsing.

This is everything a community used bookstore should be. I love it. Don’t bring your laptop when you come here, be free. Leave it behind, grab a book, bring a book, read, talk to people, be offline for a while.

There are cute dogs roaming around, Victorian-style chairs tucked into corners, and some comfy couches where you can plop down and read. The whole place feels warm, relaxed, and inviting. The owners will happily chat with you about books, art, puzzles, and anything in between.

Place: So Much More To The Story: A Bookery
Hours: ["Monday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Tuesday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Wednesday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Thursday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Friday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: We are a book store that specializes in new books and new releases. We have prepared a selection of books and related items that have been specially chosen and organized for you. Whether you are looking for yourself or a gift for a friend or family member, we have some special options for you. We are happy to recommend some things we love and hope that you love them too!
Comments:
Adorable aesthetically. Whoever designed it clearly put a lot of effort into making it cute. It could honestly be a set for a Hallmark movie where the main character is some hotshot lawyer or banker from New York who comes home, stops by the local bookstore to be alone with their thoughts, and ends up running into their high school sweetheart they haven’t seen since graduation. They kind of look down on them for never leaving the small hometown, but they’re so charming that they start talking, go on a first date, and things take off from there. Yeah, that’s the vibe. Hallmark movie.

The book selection definitely leans younger. I wouldn’t call it expansive, but it’s solid. There’s a couch and a few chairs, not a ton, but if you can snag one it’s a nice spot to read for a bit. There’s some outdoor seating too, so you’ve got more options when it’s warm out.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 4 PM"]
Description: Bright and lively coffee shop serving good coffee, tea, & brekky burritos all day! A colorful and playful aesthetic, warm staff, and vibrant atmosphere!
Comments:
Super small, but it’s in The Bowl at Ballantyne with plenty to do and tons of outdoor seating. If it’s warm, you won’t worry about finding a seat. The inside seating is very unique looking. I'm not good at describing things, but it just doesn't look like a typical coffee shop set up. Very colorful, curves here and there in the design. I like it. The seats aren't comfortable though, just wooden backs, but if you're looking to stay awake and focused, then they're marvelous.

If you can snag a cubicle, you can post up and get some work done, there's even some outlets. The corner ones are my favorite. Feels kind of snug. Definitely bring noise canceling headphones though since it’s small and you can hear everyone within always.

This Charlotte Observer article said to order the Cumulus, described as “an iced matcha with toasted coconut, blue spirulina, and a cloud of cream, agave, and sea salt. If you’re not a coconut fan, skip it, but otherwise, this drink is a little work of art.” 

That sounds spot on for the kind of vibe this place has. I haven't yet gotten into matcha, despite recent rather persuasive attempts to win me over. If you try it, let me know how it is.

Right across the street, also in The Bowl, you have The Salty Donut with more indoor space and cinnamon rolls. Oh, and this place has cinnamon rolls too, orange flavored. So, if you drive down here you can do a whole cinnamon roll run with Fly Kid Fly, The Salty across the street, Sunflour Bakery nearby, and Smallcakes Ballantyne. Bro that’s four places to get cinnamon rolls in a small little area. I love Ballantyne.

Hours: ["Monday: 9 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 9 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 9 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 9 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Small suburban public library branch with quiet reading areas, computers, and regular community programs for kids and adults. A good pick for focused work thanks to free Wi-Fi and plenty of outlets in a calm setting. Free parking on-site and no purchase required.
Comments:
It's a library, and it is small. To me, there are no cozy seating spots here. It's either in the middle of the floor where you're surrounded by people, or back in a corner facing a wall while people walk the book shelves behind you. Still, it's a modern library, it's nice, but I don't like the seating arrangements.

That said, this is down the street from Sunflour Baking Company, Rocky River Coffee Co., and Verb Coffee | The Burg Church, so you've got plenty of options in the area if you don't like it here. Also, there's a newish YMCA around the corner. If you have a YMCA membership, you can go to any of them in the Charlotte area. This one is nice.

Address: 3775 Concord Pkwy S Suite 116, Concord, NC 28027
Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 8:30 PM", "Saturday: 9 AM - 8:30 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Your Neighborhood Coffee and Ice Cream shop. Serving Hot and Iced Coffee, 24 flavors of Hersey's Ice Cream. Milkshakes, fresh bakery.
Comments:
Very cute place, small and compact interior, but it smells marvelous on account of the ice cream they sell. It's well adorned to be inviting and friendly with cushioned chairs here and there, various antiques like hockey tables and gumball machines, and even a kid's coloring section where they put up drawings.

Even the music they choose to play inside is very bubblegum, ice cream truck, happy, light, and sparkly. Feels like ice cream and happiness. Free Wi-Fi, not the best outlet access but there's a spot or two. There's one outdoor seating table. Lots of ice cream options, coffee, hot tea, but most importantly...

They have cinnamon rolls, and they are unique. It's neither cream cheese frosting nor traditional icing that adorns the roll. Instead, it's this caramely drizzle, I can't even say for sure it's caramel, but that's what stands in the place of the more traditional options. And it's light, the flavor isn't overwhelming, but it's not nonexistent. It's there, just existing to accentuate the flavors of the cinnamon roll without stealing the show. The result is a cinnamon roll where you can truly taste and enjoy the cinnamon. 

The icing doesn't overpower it, say for example the way any bottom tier mall variety cinnamon roll does, where they drench it in so much icing that you can't taste anything but gooey bread and sugar. That's not artistry my friends, that's not craftsmanship. Here, they opt for a lighter touch of icing, letting the cinnamon roll as a concept, as a pastry, as a craft, be the main show without distraction. I salute them.

Hours: ["Monday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 12 PM - 10 PM", "Friday: 12 PM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 12 PM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 7 PM"]
Description: Vibe Café & Bar brings a unique experience to the Dilworth Neighborhood of Charlotte, NC. Not only is it a place where you can drop by for your morning cup of coffee and breakfast, but also where you can enjoy an original, soul food inspired menu with our European Cocktails, unlike you have ever tasted before.
Comments:
Relaxing wine bar vibes in terms of the aesthetic. Soft lighting, wood tones, touches of greenery (fake plants count) here and there. Lots of Russian inspired desserts that I've never heard of but all sound good, if not interesting. They have something called a chocolate sausage. It's not what you think. I mean, I didn't order it, but I at least asked. It's a sweet treat pastry is all I'll say, no sausage involved.

Good amount of seating, most of it indoors but there's some comfy lounge chair couches outside as well. Decent access to outlets, I counted 5 spots you could sit and access one, not bad at all for a place this size. Free Wi-Fi, and it's plenty fast, so you can plop down here to work or study. There's a very popular Starbucks across the street known for being busy, whereas things are usually pretty calm here. In that sense, it's a hidden gem.

The sink in the men's bathroom is very interesting. I had to look this up, but it's called a "cantilevered glass vanity", which to normal people is a "floating glass sink". Never seen anything like it, but I mean, I don't come from wealth. Oh, and they also have nice teacups. The handle has this curvature thing to it that wraps into the cup interior, very nice.

Last note, the parking garage has a sign that says 2-hour parking only, but I talked to the staff and apparently that's not enforced at all. You can stay longer and relax. But I mean, if you do get towed, definitely contact me so I can update this comment (and sorry, that's rough buddy).

Address: 917 Central Ave unit b, Charlotte, NC 28204
Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 8:30 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 8:30 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 8:30 AM - 9 PM"]
Description: Local Eastern Cafe located inside Sip City! We offer quality house made syrups, food and good vibes. We have plenty of room with comfortable couches, FREE WIFI. So, come in for your Daily Ritual! See you soon, cheers!
Comments:
Same building as Sip City Market & Bottle Shop. So, it just depends on the time of day you go, whether it's Daily Ritual or Sip City running things.

The vibe is creative and cozy, like a mini art gallery, with paintings from local artists on the walls and various card games on the tables. Most of the seating is four-person tables, but there are comfy couches mixed in and a bit of outdoor seating.

Good outlet access, and good tea options, including pure Japanese matcha. It’s right across the street from Central Coffee, a Charlotte staple that’s fantastic but super small. If you’re in the area and need more space, this is a great alternative. Turn into the lot next to Two Scoops Creamery for parking, spots are limited though.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 5 PM"]
Description: Good vibes. Relaxed atmosphere. We specialize in matcha and espresso.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 10 PM"]
Description: Save on your favorite products and enjoy award-winning service at Publix Super Market at Arboretum Park. Shop our wide selection of high-quality meats, local produce, sustainably sourced seafood, and more. Try our signature items such as our Deli subs and Bakery cakes. Looking for something special? Our friendly associates are happy to help. Visit our Charlotte, NC store and see why shopping here is a pleasure.
Comments:
There’s a fairly large upstairs seating area on the entrance side of the building that makes this a third place. Students in the area seem to have caught on and can be found up there studying. Aside from that, it’s a Publix. Great deli (the chicken tender sub is 🔥) and hot food section, all other typical grocery items are available as well. Solid low key spot to plop down and get some work done. 

The seating feels secluded from the rest of the store on account of being upstairs. There’s even restrooms up there, so once you buy your stuff, you don’t have to go back down until you’re ready to leave. Also, access to outlets is surprisingly solid, so this is a gem all around.

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: 6:30 AM - 5:30 PM", "Wednesday: 6:30 AM - 5:30 PM", "Thursday: 6:30 AM - 5:30 PM", "Friday: 6:30 AM - 5:30 PM", "Saturday: 6:30 AM - 5:30 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Located in Waxhaw, North Carolina, Virtuoso serves artisan bread and pastries with a twist of our own signature flavor. We also serve espresso, drip coffee, iced chai, and naturally sugared soda.
Comments:
I came here for cinnamon rolls not expecting it to also be a third place. So, I was pleasantly surprised walking in to see people sitting all around with their friends and loved ones, chatting, eating pastries, drinking coffee, and enjoying life. It’s in a converted house that’s pretty small, but they manage to have enough seating that Waxhaw families seem to regularly come here to hangout. So much so that they have coloring books for kids.

It’s a bakery known for its bread. Sourdough, focaccia, brioche, croissants, and more. But idgaf about all that, I came for their cinnamon rolls. At first glance, you might mistake it for a coffee roll, which is a doughnut, not a cinnamon roll. But upon closer inspection, there’s no doubt that what they serve here is a proper cinnamon roll. 

They opt for icing instead of cream cheese frosting, which means its best enjoyed warm (you can have a room temp cinnamon roll with cream cheese frosting, and it’ll taste fine, but icing demands warmth to shine).
