Once you warm this, it’s solid. Not transcendent, but solid. I still have Honeybear Bake Shop as my top spot in the region, with Sunflour Bakery and Beyond Amazing Doughnuts right behind them. That said, there’s so many third places in Waxhaw with cinnamon rolls that the whole city is worth a day trip. Just check the map (shout out the greatest map of all time).

Anyways, they also serve coffee and tea and have Wi-Fi, so if you can snag a spot, it’s not a bad place to study, work, or read. Just bring noise canceling headphones because it’s small to the point of being able to hear everyone in the house at all times.

Address: 116 E South Main St, Waxhaw, NC 28173
Hours: ["Monday: 6 AM - 7 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 7 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 7 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 7 PM", "Friday: 6 AM - 7 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 7 PM"]
Description: The Daily Grind Coffee Bar serves Charlotte-based Night Swim Coffee, including espresso drinks and coffee flights. The menu includes smoothies and baked goods such as doughnuts, muffins, macarons, cake pops, and other pastries.
Comments: Small but they use the space well, and there's a bunch of benches outside for more seating. Good spot to check out on a warm day. Right in the heart of downtown Waxhaw, so within walking distance to a bunch of shops and other third places. Check out the map, downtown Waxhaw feels like it could be a set for a Hallmark movie. Very walkable, lots of store fronts, friendly people, good food.

Hours: ["Monday: 7:30 AM - 7 PM", "Tuesday: 7:30 AM - 7 PM", "Wednesday: 7:30 AM - 7 PM", "Thursday: 7:30 AM - 7 PM", "Friday: 7:30 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 7:30 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 7:30 AM - 7 PM"]
Description: The Salty Donut, an artisan donut & coffee shop.
Comments:
Cool spot in an even cooler location (The Bowl at Ballantyne). The interior is small in terms of seating, but not compact. It doesn't feel cramped, and there are some larger tables where groups can sit. On warm days, there's a good amount of outdoor seating, so you can be reasonably sure you'll find somewhere to sit coming here. Outlet access is challenging, so charge your laptop if you're coming to work or study. 

Kind of a pricey menu, but that’s just the world these days. Blame inflation. Parking is free if you can snag a street spot and have the confidence to parallel park. If not, there’s a shared lot for all the businesses at The Bowl that gives you a few free hours. After that, you’ve got to pay.

Now, onto a topic of the utmost importance. They’ve got what is officially called a Honey Butter Cinnabun on their menu. Close enough. I’m counting it as a cinnamon roll. And it’s good, really good. Still, it leans more toward a donut than a traditional cinnamon roll. You don’t get that heavy, weighty interior with distinct coils that you could unravel. There's not that clear picture of something having been rolled up to create its present form.

You couldn’t pull this apart from spiral to straight. It’s all baked together like a soft bread roll. But it’s still delicious. So, it sort of borders on “can we really call this a cinnamon roll?” And to be fair, they do call them cinnabuns, not cinnamon rolls. Maybe for good reason. I'm certainly overthinking this. I am to cinnamon rolls what a pedant is to literature. I have issues. Ignore my ranting, their cinnabun is good, you should try it.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 7 PM"]
Description: The Salty Donut, an artisan donut & coffee shop.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 5 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 5 PM"]
Description: Specialty coffee, local pastries, craft beer and wine.
Comments: Compact interior, but what space they have, they use exceptionally well. There's a surprising amount of seating packed into such a small space, and it's done with superb access to outlets. There's some outdoor seating to spill over into if it's packed inside, which happens quite often here. It's a popular spot in a very busy business park. Like, seriously. In the mornings every sort of business looking person in the area is meeting here for coffee chats or to work remotely the first half of the day. Come early to secure the best spots. Parking is in a garage shared by the businesses in the area. Drive around, you'll find it.

Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: Tous les Jours means "every day"in French. Tous les Jours is a "French-Asian-inspired" bakery primarily serving a selection of baked goods and beverages.
Comments:
I've been told the Wi-Fi password is printed at the bottom of your receipt if you choose to get one. Otherwise, there’s a small sticky note near the checkout counter with it. Ask the staff if you can’t find it, although as an introvert myself, there’s a mild amount of social anxiety I get when asking for the Wi-Fi password only for the barista to point to a big obvious sign I somehow missed. So, yeah.

The layout here is pretty neat. There’s stadium-style seating with pillows, some lounge chairs, and a few tables with chairs both upstairs and downstairs. The main challenge is the truly abysmal access to outlets. If you’re planning to work here, charge your laptop ahead of time. Other than that, I like the vibes. The pastries are solid. Honestly, better than Amelie’s in my opinion.

Also, when Tous les Jours first came to Charlotte, I was like “wow, what a cool spot.” Then a friend from New York was like “lol, y’all really think this is special?” and I was embarrassed (NYC transplants in Charlotte please just go home if nothing here pleases or impresses you, stop bullying the rest of us for being simple and happy). Turns out they’re a chain. Not that that should change your opinion of them, just don’t go thinking it’s Charlotte local.

Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: Closed", "Thursday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 9 PM"]
Description: Pho, Boba Tea, Mochi Donut, Vietnamese Sandwiches , Korean Pastries and Cakes, Korean Corn Dog, Vietnamese Street Foods. “One stop shop” where you can spend a “date out” with friends and family gather together to create more memories.
Comments:
Lots of Asian food options here alongside bubble tea, mochi donuts, coffee and tea. They have plenty of seating and Wi-Fi, although very few outlets, so if you're coming with your laptop, have it charged and ready. There's a Publix and Walmart in the same complex so you can get some grocery shopping done afterwards.

Also, it smells very much like an Asian food store. Very authentic, like everything that comes out of the kitchen was made by someone whose been making it their whole life. Like it's someone's aunt or uncle or grandma or something in there just going to work. The food tastes as good as it smells. Sample the menu, it's all good.

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: Closed", "Wednesday: Closed", "Thursday: 10 AM - 5 PM", "Friday: 10 AM - 5 PM", "Saturday: 10 AM - 2 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Black-owned local bakery and cafe doling out coffee, tea, cakes, cookies, and other treats in a cozy space with live jazz on Sundays.
Comments:
A hidden gem in the truest sense. Really good pound cakes, but what stands out are the owners, Marilyn and Sam. They've made something special with this place. The live jazz on Sundays, the way Marilyn remembers everyone's name, the fact that they're only open three days a week because this is clearly a passion project. People from Cornelius call them 'treasures within the community' and that's exactly right. 

Yes, it's small. Yes, the hours are limited. But that's precisely what makes it a gem. I wouldn't ever come here to work on my laptop. Leave it behind, talk to people, maybe grab a corner and read a book, listen to some jazz, chat with some boomers, and brush it off when they start asking why you're still single and how they have a really nice grandchild you should meet 😅.

Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 7 PM"]
Description:
Our journey began with a love for bubble tea and a desire to share it with others. We saw an opportunity to bring something new and exciting, and we jumped at the chance to start our own business.

Our mission is to provide a high-quality and delicious drink option. We take pride in using only authentic ingredients and the highest quality tea leaves in our drinks. We believe that bubble tea should be more than just a drink - it should be an experience. That's why we strive to create a welcoming and friendly atmosphere for our customers.
Comments: Extremely compact, and yet for the space they manage to fit 6-7 two person tables. You’ll be sitting on top of the person next to you, but there is indoor seating, plus a few outdoor spots, so you can hangout if you’d like. Free Wi-Fi indicates they encourage and expect people to stay awhile. Their menu is a marvelous hodgepodge of fairly priced overly sweet items, including bubble waffles, ice cream, all sorts of iced teas, and my favorite, hot teas that you can get without sugar.

Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 10 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 9 PM"]
Description: Taiwanese-style bubble tea chain serving milk teas, fruit teas, and blended drinks with classic boba and other toppings. Drinks are highly customizable, and peak times can get busy with after-school and weekend crowds. Easy strip-mall parking.

Hours: ["Monday: 10 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 10 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 10 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 10 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 10 AM - 10:30 PM", "Saturday: 10 AM - 10:30 PM", "Sunday: 10 AM - 10 PM"]
Description: Taiwanese-style bubble tea shop with a large menu of milk teas, fruit teas, and ice-blended drinks plus plenty of topping options. Great for grab-and-go, but expect a line during evenings and weekends. Free parking in the surrounding retail lot.
Comments:
I first came here after trying to visit nearby Cocotte on a Saturday morning and finding it so packed I could barely get in the door. Which, of course, is a testament to how great that place is. But I’m not a fan of crowds, so I walked here instead. Literally took 2 minutes.

As for the place itself, it has all the little amenities you’d want. A wide range of cold and hot tea options with different combinations and varying sugar and ice levels, a board game cabinet, a “Joy Catcher” claw vending machine thing, a medium amount of space, fast and free Wi‑Fi, and around 3 to 4 seating spots with good access to outlets. It was just me and the staff. I was happy.

Aesthetically, it’s very clean and polished, like the building itself is new. Their background music is good. They also have a tablet you can order on if you really don’t want to talk to the staff. But it’s right next to the actual counter, so it kind of feels rude to not talk to the person watching you place an order they’re about to go make anyway. But idk man, maybe that’s great for super introverts. I consider myself moderately introverted.

Description: Sharetea is the most authentic Boba Tea brand from Taiwan! Sharetea's Boba Tea, or called Bubble Tea are made from high quality tea leaves and selected ingredients shipped directly from Taiwan. All Sharetea's products are 100% guaranteed to arrive fresh and tasty.

Hours: ["Monday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Friday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 12 PM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 9 PM"]
Description: Sharetea Steele Creek is where bubble tea fans and coffee enthusiasts unite in a charming space at 4922 Trojan Dr. Launched on November 4, 2024, this Charlotte hotspot offers an extensive selection of delicious bubble tea and coffee beverages along with pastries and snacks. Whether you're seeking a refreshing treat or a cozy spot to sip and relax, Sharetea Steele Creek welcomes you into a world of taste and serenity.
Comments:
It smells marvelous in here. Not sure what it is, but it’s a sweet and light aroma. Maybe it was just the day I went, idk. Smelled really good though. The place is small, but they managed to fit two stuffed animal vending machine claw game things. 

It has a very muted, minimalist aesthetic. Clean and uniform. Grays, whites, gold accents. It’s right next to a Wing Stop and Olive Garden, so if you’re hungry, get a drink here, get your food nearby. 

There’s Wi-Fi and 2 spots you can sit and reach an outlet, so you could come here to work or study. I wouldn’t though, seems like it’d be hard to focus. The entire store is always in view no matter where you sit. Good spot for meeting friends though, and drinking bubble tea if you’re into that.

Description: Dessert-forward teahouse known for chewy mochi donuts alongside bubble tea and coffee drinks. Donut flavors tend to rotate and popular ones can sell out, so earlier visits pay off. Free parking nearby, with a casual, quick-stop vibe.
Comments: Beautiful aesthetic, floral sort of thing going on, modern finish, smells very good. Bubble tea and donuts, plenty of flavors. And despite having really good access to outlets, they don’t have free wi-fi, so this is a no go for studying or remote work. Still worth visiting though if you just want to hangout, read a book, drink some tea, chat with friends, etc. Or if you’re willing to burn through your hotspot 🤣.

Description: Cabarrus County Public Library's Concord branch offers dedicated spaces for different age groups including a teen section with anime collection and an upstairs children's area separated from adult sections. Open Monday through Saturday, the library provides traditional services including book lending, community programs, and summer reading initiatives.

Hours: ["Monday: 9 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 9 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 9 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 9 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 10 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Connecting citizens with information & resources that educate, enrich, and empower.
Comments:
I love libraries. I didn't love this one. For a building so large, there's hardly anywhere to sit. And the book selection is rather limited. Now, what's great about this place is its location. As in, its proximity to other places that are better to hang out at. Downtown Concord has a lot, just check the map!

And to keep it 💯, a lot of people from Concord say this library feels more like a homeless shelter to them. I mean, they don't really bother anyone, but they're present throughout, so worth calling out. The kids' section is upstairs though and very clearly set up so that if you're not walking in with kids, it's like why are you up here, which I think is good design.

Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 6 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 6 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 6 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 6 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 6 PM"]
Description: Airy cafe/used bookshop benefiting Habitat for Humanity offers free WiFi & seating in a loft.
Comments:
It’s really nice to see a place that isn’t just industrial chic, super modern all-white "doctor’s office", or another cliché coffee shop aesthetic. Instead, it feels both clean and inviting, but also lived in. The upstairs loft is honestly one of my favorite places in Charlotte to read a book. There isn’t a ton of seating, but if you snag a spot, you can nestle in for hours, put your cares aside, and just enjoy some literature. Every so often, you can glance up at the huge art piece on the wall for something to look at.

Downstairs is the bookstore itself. It’s not huge and the selection isn’t on a Barnes & Noble level, but it feels authentic. The books really come from the community. Kids donate their grandma’s collections, students finishing school pay it forward, people leave their favorite titles because they have extra copies, and so on. The collection here feels more heartfelt than at other bookstores in the city.

They have coffee, tea, snacks, grab and go food, a few pastries and cookies, and most importantly, good vibes. There is usually a great early 2000s easy listening playlist. Think The Script, Kodaline, John Mayer, The Fray, Kelly Clarkson, Plain White T’s, Snow Patrol, Five for Fighting. All good stuff.

As a bonus, the shop connects internally to a Habitat for Humanity store, so you can do a little shopping after reading. Check their website for more details about the Habitat for Humanity connection.

Description: Coffee shop offering specialty coffee, fresh pastries, and light breakfast and lunch options made daily. The cozy space features indoor and outdoor seating, with a quiet atmosphere suitable for working on laptops or casual dining. Popular menu items include various lattes, matcha drinks, and waffles, with kitchen service available until 3PM most days.

Description: Verb Coffee operates this location inside Multiply Church, serving specialty coffee and pastries with limited hours primarily during mornings and early afternoons. The coffee shop offers dine-in and takeout options with free parking available in the church lot. Known for consistent quality coffee and friendly baristas, this location provides a quiet workspace atmosphere.

Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Verb Coffee operates this location inside The Burg Church in Harrisburg, serving specialty coffee, house-made pastries, and light fare with local ingredients. The family-friendly spot features a fenced playground for children and offers indoor seating with a quiet atmosphere suitable for work. Known for friendly service and quality coffee drinks including lattes and matcha options.
Comments:
If you've ever seen The Good Place or Bruce Almighty, this feels like a coffee shop straight out of their versions of heaven. It's so bright, pure-looking, and impeccably clean that it goes beyond the typical modern coffee shop aesthetic. It's as if someone deliberately designed it to resemble a café in heaven, or at least how heaven is often portrayed in movies and TV. The atmosphere is calming, and the space is airy, it doesn't feel like everyone is on top of each other. 

Outlet access is fantastic. I'm pretty sure you can charge your device from almost every single seat inside. But here's what really won me over. The men's restroom has complimentary mouthwash, Q-tips, band-aids, lotion, a lint roller, and body spray. As a man, I've spent a lifetime accepting that men's restrooms are usually borderline post-apocalyptic, while women's restrooms typically have luxury touches to them. This place actually cares about the men’s restroom experience, and I respect that. We deserve it Kings 👑.

Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 10 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: A locally owned shop serving Asian cuisine & specialty bubble tea.
Comments:
It's small and the chairs are uncomfortable, but the food is good, and it's usually not that busy outside of the lunch rush. So, it's a good place to plop down and have a meal with some friends, or get some work done quickly. The food is actually the highlight to me here, it's just in a corner of Charlotte nobody really goes to unless they work in the area, so it's not well known.

Also, I'm not into bubble tea but they have plenty of it, and it looks good. I refrain from participating in the bubble tea craze because I don't want to end up liking it and add all that sugar to my regular diet.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Saturday: Closed", "Sunday: 8 AM - 9:30 AM, 11:15 AM - 12 PM"]
Description: Stop by The Gathering at Calvary Church for delicious brewed coffee, specialty beverages, and small bites! Just inside Calvary's main entrance Atrium, you'll find the café—a warm and welcoming space filled with natural light. Perfect for meeting friends and enjoying conversation and community.
Comments:
This place is a great excuse to visit Calvary Church, even if you're not religious, simply because of how beautiful it is. The external architecture alone is worth the trip. From the outside, it’s stunning. And on the inside, it’s massive, pristine, and modern in ways that surprised me. Calvary is known for being more traditional, the suit and tie kind of church, but the space feels fresh.

The coffee shop is tucked in a corner near the entrance. On its own, with both indoor and outdoor seating, it’s pretty spacious. And if it gets full, there’s plenty of extra lounge-style seating in the lobby that feels fair game for coffee shop patrons. There’s also a Christian bookstore and reading area called the Word Room that’s just as large as the shop itself.

As for the coffee, I’m not a coffee drinker, so I won’t pretend to be an expert, but it didn't appear to be anything remarkable. The tea options are pretty standard too, just your usual bagged selections. But the real draw here is the atmosphere. The peace and quiet, the beauty of the church, the solid access to outlets, the little umbrella-covered tables outside, and the chance to walk around the campus without needing to attend a service.

I'm a Christian, but non-denominational, so I probably wouldn’t have ended up at Calvary otherwise. I’m glad I came to see the coffee shop and explore the area. It’s a cool space.

Place: Carolina Beer Temple & the Ames Street Marketplace
Address: 215 N Ames St Suite 1000, Matthews, NC 28105
Hours: ["Monday: 2 PM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 2 PM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 2 PM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 12 PM - 11 PM", "Friday: 12 PM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 12 PM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 12 PM - 8 PM"]
Description: Beer & bottle shop with rotating taps of craft brews plus hundreds of options by the can & bottle.
Comments:
Brewery with a bunch of smaller businesses inside that altogether comprise of Ames Market. Be sure to check out Two Scoops Creamery, and the bakery Innescas Sweets of Europe right around the corner. 

They have live music and family friendly pop up events every so often. There’s also arcade style games and a cannabis dispensary inside if you’re into that. It’s a nice area, park once and you’ve got plenty to check out and explore.

Description: Innesca’s Sweets of Europe – A European-inspired bakery in Matthews, NC specializing in delicate pastries, artisanal cakes, and gourmet coffee. From French macarons and mousse cakes to classic Eastern European desserts, every treat is made fresh with high-quality ingredients. Visit us for a taste of Europe, right in the heart of Matthews downtown.
Comments:
You know how doctor’s offices usually have a very white, polished, pristine aesthetic? This place has that, but with an elegance that makes it significantly more pleasant to hangout in. Idk if that’s a great description. My point is, it’s a beautiful space. Small, but well designed.

They serve coffee and tea, but as a bakery, their pastries are what stand out the most. They sometimes have cinnamon rolls, but they sell out early, so if you want one, wake up on time. They’ve also got a sweet cheese roll that’s pretty good, and ice cream cone shaped cake pops that taste as good as they look. 

And as an area, it’s a cool place to hangout, as there's a brewery in the same complex with lots of seating and family friendly events every so often. Together, they’re a great addition to the Matthews third place scene.

Description: At Platform Coffee, we’re all about people, building community, making connections, and serving exceptional coffee along the way. We roast all our coffee right here in Charlotte, so every cup is as fresh as it gets. Whether you’re stopping in for your daily go-to or meeting up with a friend, our team is here to welcome you with genuine hospitality and make every visit feel special.
Comments:
Same company as Platform Coffee + Kitchen but this is a different location in Matthews. Not much in the way of food options (hence the name Platform Coffee, not Coffee + Kitchen) but they have pastries, including rather good cinnamon rolls 🙌🏾🔥. There’s plenty of seating despite being a medium sized space, with good access to outlets at seats along the walls. It’s attached to New City Church internally, so they’re closed on Sundays, but any other day you’re good to go. 

Visually, it’s an industrial chic aesthetic, with the typical unfinished floor, but they go for black tables and chairs with blue and green pops of color here and there, rather than the all white doctor’s office look many similar spots prefer. Nothing standout, but it’s a nice place.

If you read the comments on this website, you’ll notice I never talk about the quality of coffee at places. I don’t drink coffee, I’m an herbal tea guy, and I stay in my lane. They have some solid herbal tea options here, so I was happy. I’m sure the coffee is great though, check the Google Maps reviews for more there.

Hours: ["Monday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Friday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 6 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 6 PM"]
Description: Coffeehouse chain serving signature drinks, mochas & lattes, plus coffee & breakfast.
Comments:
The nicest Caribou Coffee I’ve ever been to. Pristine, lots of space, solid outlet access, even the couch next to the faux fireplace has an outlet. Wooden floors, tables, and most chairs, but it’s that bright colored wood that looks like it was born yesterday.

Caribou has great sandwiches and an XL size for drinks that’s larger than the Starbucks Venti, so I always prefer their locations when they’re nearby. This one is lovely. Top tier study or remote work spot for those in the area. I wouldn’t say it’s a must visit for a day trip from Charlotte since it’s a chain, but if you’re nearby, it’s worth hanging out at for sure.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 6 PM"]
Description: Established cafe offering sandwiches, salads & breakfast, plus unique sweet teas & espresso drinks.
Comments:
Good access to outlets, especially along the tall chair bar-style seating. Someone clearly put effort into making sure people could stay charged up. Coffee Republic is expanding steadily across the Charlotte area, and I welcome it. All of their locations are quality.

This spot sits inside a converted industrial building with wooden floors, old brick walls, and stained wood finishes. It even smells like it, in the best way. They’ve jazzed it up with eclectic art, paintings, and little trinkets throughout. It’s a pretty neat space, and the surrounding downtown area is charming. Easy to walk around and explore local shops if you make the trip. Definitely worth a day trip from Charlotte.

Description: Specialty cafe and coffee roaster, offering hand crafted espresso drinks with fresh in-house roasted coffees from all over the world. Free wi-fi, plentiful seating, house made food and pastries.
Comments:
Calming space that leans into natural lighting, making it feel both comfortable and elegant. This is industrial chic at its best. The staff is warm and friendly. There are plenty of spots to sit, plug in, and settle in for work or study.

Solid tea options too. The Blueberry Rooibos is excellent. They also have coffee and food, and you can tell the owner put real effort into making this feel like a true third place. The layout invites you to linger. The booth-style seating is lined with soft oversized pillows, making it easy to sit a while without back pain. Greatly appreciated.

Description: RegalCha is a vibrant tea and coffee shop in Mooresville, NC, offering a wide variety of boba teas, fruit teas, smoothies, and specialty coffee drinks. Whether you’re craving a refreshing milk tea, a flavorful fruit blend, or a rich iced coffee, we’ve got something for everyone. We use premium ingredients and delicious toppings to deliver a fun and satisfying experience every time. Whether you're stopping by for your daily caffeine fix or a sweet treat with friends, RegalCha is your go-to spot for drinks with a royal twist.

Description:
Welcome to Sugar & Sip Coffee and Workshop, where craft coffee meets community. We’re more than just a café, we’re a creative space serving specialty coffee, espresso drinks, and signature brews made with locally roasted beans. Whether you're here for a quiet morning pour-over or a bold iced latte, every cup is crafted with care.

Looking to deepen your coffee knowledge? Join one of our hands-on workshops, from barista basics to latte art and home brewing mastery, we offer immersive experiences for coffee lovers of all levels. Stop by for coffee, connection, and creativity, or explore our shop online to bring the café experience home.
Comments:
I like this place. Small but full of understated personality, and black-owned👏🏾 (the person behind this website is black). They have these pod seating things by the entrance that look cool. Very comfy. And it smells marvelous here, I believe because of a candle they have going somewhere. Limited menu compared to other coffee shops in Charlotte, but what they do serve, they serve well.

The interior design is very well done. Earthy, muted tones, a sort of modern minimalism, lots of natural light from floor to ceiling windows, a thoughtful orange-red accent wall bearing their logo, mid-century modern wooden chairs paired with cushioned benches. It's not your cookie cutter coffee shop design wise, someone put thought into making this place feel unique, reflect their personality, and they did a wonderful job.

Description:
August Lily Florist proudly serving the Monroe, North Carolina area since 2009, offering a wide variety of fresh floral arrangements and gifts to suit any style, occasion and budget. We're happy to assist you with local and worldwide deliveries, including daily deliveries to local funeral homes and hospitals.

We are a family-owned local business that is dedicated to the well being of our community. Our shop is a fresh new twist on an old local building. We have renovated a 100 year old residence on Benton Street to house fresh flowers, silk flowers, plants, artwork and an assortment of gifts.

Address: 9325 Jw Clay Blvd Suite 223, Charlotte, NC 28262
Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 11 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 11 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 11 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 11 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 1 AM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 1 AM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 11 PM"]
Description:
Discover the authentic taste of Yemeni coffee at Qamaria Coffee in University City, Charlotte, just minutes from the UNC Charlotte campus. We specialize in traditional Yemeni drinks like qishr and brewed coffee using freshly roasted beans and ancient brewing techniques.

Whether you’re a student, faculty member, or coffee lover, our cozy café offers a perfect space to study, relax, or catch up with friends. Located near JW Clay Blvd and the light rail station, we bring a unique Middle Eastern coffeehouse experience to the heart of Charlotte. Visit us today for rich flavors, welcoming vibes, and unforgettable coffee.
Comments:
A welcome addition to growing Charlotte Middle Eastern coffee shop scene which, as of 2025, includes Qahwah House, Haraz Elizabeth, Haraz LoSo, Vavela Cafe, Anatolia Cafe & Cuisine, and now Qamaria.

Haraz is rumored to be opening some new locations, and a spot called Bayt Al-Mocha is on its way to town sometime soon. They're all wonderful, although I still give Vavela the award for most beautiful coffee shop in Charlotte. I’d say this spot is most similar to Qahwah House in terms of size, and it shares the same beautiful, polished, upscale aesthetic that all the Middle Eastern coffee shops seem to have. The floors here literally glisten. 

One thing that stands out is the booth-style seating, with soft patterned fabric and pillows that make it easy to stay awhile and study, read, or work remotely. The cheesecake options are crazy in the best way possible. They don’t have cinnamon rolls, but they do sometimes have a cinnamon roll cheesecake, a revolutionary combination of pastry proportions I'd never imagined possible.

If you like any of the other Middle Eastern coffee spots, you should make your way here. It’s worth the drive no matter what side of town you’re on. Outlet access is solid if you grab a spot by the wall. Most of the seating is round tables in the center, so come with a full charge just in case. Parking is free in the shared garage behind the building.

Description:
At Sugar Creek Coffee Roasters, we specialize in hand crafted, small batch, artisan roasted coffee. We don't roast your coffee beans until you place an order, so you can be assured the freshest roasted coffee is delivered to your door.
​
Located inside Sweet Union Brewing in Indian Trail, North Carolina, we're proud to support the local economy, as well as ship freshly roasted beans across the United States. We have a growing list of partnerships, including breweries and coffee shops, as well as several non-profit organizations that benefit from our support.

Address: 5904 Indian Trail Fairview Rd, Indian Trail, NC 28079
Hours: ["Monday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Saturday: 6:30 AM - 8 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Welcome to Psalms Coffee, your local cafe in Indian Trail, where we combine smalltown Christian values with delectable foods and beverages to deliver the ultimate coffee shop experience. As a family-owned coffee shop, we cordially invite you to stop by and treat yourself to some freshly-brewed coffee, healthy chai tea, thirst-quenching lemonade, or palate-pleasing frappuccino.
Comments:
Let me start by confessing my bias. I’m a devout Christian, so naturally I’m partial to this place. I love the Psalms and often turn to them as a way to enter into prayer. So when I walked in and saw the name, the concept, and the unapologetically Christian theme, I couldn’t help but love the place. 

There are Bible verses throughout the shop, a warm and inviting decor, and a wooden cross where you can leave a prayer or note on a post-it. I saw open Bibles on tables and overheard conversations about faith. Everything about this place resonated with me. To be clear though, it’s open to everyone. The owners just aren’t shy about their faith, which is exactly how it should be in a country founded in part on religious freedom.

It’s small, and especially popular on weekends, but for good reason. It’s new as of 2025 and already has a steady group of regulars. It’s miraculous I’ve talked this much and haven’t yet mentioned the fact that they have cinnamon rolls 🙌🏾🔥. Well sized, generously iced, filled with cinnamon goodness. They also have other pastries and cold drinks, along with a full coffee menu and a few tea options.

If you have kids, there’s a fenced-in play area right out front so you can sip coffee while your little ones run around without worry. It’s a thoughtfully designed coffee shop, unambiguous in its Christian mission and commitment to charity, kindness, and making all feel welcome. I love it.

Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 2:30 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 2:30 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 2:30 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 2:30 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 2:30 PM", "Saturday: Closed", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Finca Coffee is a coffee shop in the lobby of Eastgate Charlotte church. Our goal is to provide the best farm-to-cup experience and create a space in the community that has a relaxed vibe perfect for work, study or just catching up with friends.
Comments:
Very clean, bright, and modern. It’s inside a church that’s been around for a while, but the coffee shop is newer and pristine as a result. Nice place to get some work done. It’s well-lit, and they have those metal chairs with rounded legs and the skinny back support piece with a solid plate in the middle. The kind it seems every business in Charlotte uses. Functional, but not exactly cozy, so you won’t be dozing off.

I don’t drink coffee, even though I run a website that catalogs almost every single shop in Charlotte. So no comments there. But they have a few tea options, and they were sufficient.

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 2 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 4 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 4 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 4 PM", "Saturday: Closed", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Sunflower Club is a gluten-free café offering fresh Belgian waffles, gourmet coffee, teas, and local baked goods. Enjoy art by local creatives while supporting outreach programs and wellness retreats through your visit. No membership required to dine, and both café and farm locations are available for event rentals.

Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 4 PM"]
Description: Deli St is an innovative concept aiming to offer healthy quick meal options for young professionals and neighborhood residents that are always on the go. In a hip neighborhood full of diverse eateries, breweries and coffee shops Deli St draws the difference by baking their own goods, offering vegetarian and non-vegetarian options as well as a full coffee bar.
Comments:
If you're vegan or have vegan friends, you'll love this spot. Lots of great vegan sandwiches and health-conscious choices. They have a full menu with non-vegan options too, but it's all good. The Hangover breakfast sandwich is spectacular. It's a real meal, properly sized, worth the price, and will actually fill you up.

The aesthetic is very much deli. Not a ton of seating, most of it is bar-style, but there’s pretty decent access to outlets throughout. Not a bad place to plop down with a laptop and get some work done. They at least have high chairs with backs for the bar-style seating, which helps. Feels kind of hipster, in the best way possible. I grew up in Seattle. I like it.

Hours: ["Monday: 10 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 10 AM - 7:30 PM", "Wednesday: Closed", "Thursday: 10 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 10 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 10 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: This cozy establishment offers an extensive menu of authentic bubble teas, Vietnamese coffee, and fresh bánh mì sandwiches.
Comments:
Very quiet in the evenings. I posted up here to get some work done on a random Tuesday, was nice. Small, indoor seating for 8 people, maybe 10 max, with a bit more outside. But there's Wi-Fi, food, coffee, pastries, and a lot of different bubble (or boba, whichever you prefer) tea options. Like, a lot. If that's your thing, you'll like this place.

They also have some board games available, so it's clear they welcome people to stay a while and relax. No one has ever recommended this place to me. I stumbled across it in one of my routine "type keywords into Google Maps and find random places" sessions, so I'd say it's a hidden gem.

Tags: Great Date Spot,Latino Owned,Charlotte Local,Dog Friendly
Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 7 PM"]
Description: Sumaq is a cafe inspired by Quechua, the language and culture of indigenous South America. It serves craft coffee, matcha lattes, artisanal pastries, and fresh, made-from-scratch sandwiches with culturally inspired names, with both indoor seating and an outdoor patio for remote work or casual meetups.
Comments:
They have great plant work going on inside. The food is really good, particularly the breakfast flatbread and chicken/bacon sandwich, and the cheesecake, and the empanadas. All the food is good. And sometimes, usually in the winter, they sell pumpkin flavored cinnamon rolls 👀🔥.

The vibes are good as well. Bright aesthetic, wood colored tables and chairs with green accents here and there by way of plants. Very popular spot for friend groups to meet up. Also a hot spot among remote workers. And dates, always seems to be some love birds in here on weekends.

Outlet access is good, not great though, so I’d suggest coming charged up. In 2025 they added more seating, which is awesome because the only concern here before was it’s so cool it gets pretty packed. That’s less of a problem now, easier to find a seat.

Tags: Loose Leaf Tea,Great Date Spot,Charlotte Local,Dog Friendly
Hours: ["Monday: 7:30 AM - 9 PM", "Tuesday: 7:30 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 7:30 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 7:30 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 7:30 AM - 10 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 10 PM", "Sunday: 8:30 AM - 6 PM"]
Description: Specialty coffee shop, craft beer, wine cocktail bar as well as local pastries, free live music.
Comments:
A whole vibe. Laid back lounge aesthetic, dim lighting, upscale semi-rustic decor, suave jazzy music, quite nice. Lovely place to read a book or get some work done. Try the orange flavored cinnamon rolls. Also the cookies, courtesy of Honeybear Bake Shop. Both are splendid.

They also boast marvelous herbal tea options. Of the properly steeped loose leaf variety, none of that bagged nonsense. The Mémoire Citron Pressi, an oolong apple blend with heather and red cornflower blossoms, is quite good. Just look at the name. It can’t sound like that and not be some Grade A level stuff.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 7 PM"]
Description: ¡Descubre los auténticos sabores de Colombia en Roasted Tinto! Te invitamos a disfrutar de una experiencia gastronómica única que celebra las ricas tradiciones culinarias de Colombia. En nuestro establecimiento, ofrecemos una deliciosa selección de platos icónicos como arepas, empanadas, maduros, guacamole y mucho más. Cada bocado te transportará a la esencia de nuestra cultura y sazón. ¡Ven y déjate enamorar por la auténtica cocina colombiana!
Comments:
So, the descriptions for places on this site usually come from Google Maps. The fact that this one is in Spanish tells you all you need to know about its authenticity. I don’t even know what it says, but I spent a few hours here working remotely, and almost everyone who walked in was from Latin America and spoke with the staff in Spanish.

If you walk into a spot meant to represent a certain culture and it’s full of people just like you, not from that culture, then you're in the wrong place. It ain't authentic b. This place is the opposite of that. It’s the real deal. It's tucked inside a laundromat but has its own entrance and corner. It’s small but welcoming, with bright colors that reflect the Colombian heritage of the owners. There’s some outdoor seating if it gets crowded, though I haven’t seen that be a problem.

They’ve got great food, fresh pastries, snacks, cold drinks, coffee, and some limited tea options. The empanadas are solid. A true third place, full of life and cultural pride.

Tags: Loose Leaf Tea,Great Date Spot,Charlotte Local
Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 8 PM"]
Description:
The Percantile and Creamery is the premier Coffee, Ice Cream, and Dessert Bar in the Charlotte metro area. We are thrilling people with our amazing interior design, our incredible customer service, and the quality of our offerings.

We have a beautifully designed 1920’s Mercantile-themed monochromatic throwback, where people feel excited by their surroundings, and blown away by their treats. Great for business meetings, families, couples, and you!
Comments:
Beautifully designed interior. Really beautiful art piece to your right on the wall as you walk in. Inviting atmosphere, there’s a seating area with a half wall that I love. Makes you feel nestled in, great for remote work or studying. They have excellent herbal tea options that they steep properly (no bags, loose leaf👏🏾), coffee, and all sorts of sweet treats, including ice cream and pastries.

But the topic of the utmost importance here is their cinnamon rolls. They’re a creamery in addition to a coffee shop, and some of that skill with sweetness has made its way to their cinnamon rolls. The icing (not cream cheese, which is good but harder to get right) is caked on top, in a way that provides enjoyment cold as well as warm. It’s not terribly large, not terribly wide, not terribly tall, not terribly small. It’s just right. Bravo.

If you’re looking for coffee or ice cream commentary, check the Google Maps reviews. People love both here, while I stick to herbal teas and cinnamon rolls. A man can only have so many vices. 

Lastly, if you’re super introverted or shy, they have an app and self-order kiosks where you can make your choice, sit down, and have it brought to you. I’m a software engineer by trade, so I love the tech integration.

Tags: Loose Leaf Tea,Great Date Spot,Charlotte Local
Address: 363 Church St N Suite 100, Concord, NC 28025
Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 3 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 3 PM"]
Description:
Welcome to The Perc at The Nook, a café where caffeine and comfort harmonize. Nestled within the enchanting Percent Beer, our second location invites you to indulge in expertly crafted lattes, aromatic coffee, and a symphony of teas. 

While savoring your preferred beverage, dive into our delectable light bites, designed to complement our invigorating drinks. Our inviting ambiance extends both indoors and out, with ample seating, reliable Wi-Fi, and convenient parking. At The Perc at The Nook, we create a cozy refuge for you to unwind, gather, and enjoy the convergence of coffee and community.
Comments:
Plenty of seating, enough spots with outlets to feel relatively comfortable about being able to plug in if you’re coming to work or study. All the standard coffee shop options, but they stand out for their sweet treats, specifically the ice cream. It’s wonderful. 

Cool quirky aesthetic blended with upscale industrial chic vibes. Artwork from local musicians on the walls, Indie/alternative playlist, mismatching chair and pillow colors. It’s well designed for sure.

I’m sure the coffee is great, but I’m a tea guy, so I love that they properly steep their herbal teas here. Instead of the typical coffee shop routine of rushing hot water over a bag and calling it a day, this place opts for true tea tradition, steeping the leaves at the right temperature based on their type. 

White teas are best steeped around 160°F to 180°F for 2 to 4 minutes. Green teas shine at 175°F to 185°F for 1 to 3 minutes. Black teas need a hotter brew, closer to 200°F to 212°F for 3 to 5 minutes. They understand that here. Bravo to them. Waterbean is one of the few other places in Charlotte that does as well.

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: Closed", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 3 PM"]
Description: Eatery offering gourmet sandwiches & artisanal baked goods, along with soups, omelets & pancakes.
Comments:
The first time I came here, the regulars were deep in conversation with a staff member, swapping life updates like old friends. One had been in a minor accident, another had just gotten a new car, and someone else was looking for debate on which wine to try next. Off in the corner, a guy was on his laptop, ostensibly getting some work done. That was it. I instantly pulled out my phone to add this place to the website. That’s a third place. Everyone felt at home, away from home.

The space is small, but there’s some outdoor seating that makes it reasonably comfortable on a warm day if you’re looking to plop down and hang out. Parking is on the surrounding residential streets. It’s free but can be sparse during peak times.

Bakery items, a full food menu, wine, cold drinks, and friendly staff. All the ingredients needed to settle in and stay awhile.

Tags: Great Date Spot,Hidden Gem,Charlotte Local,Dog Friendly
Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 10 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 10 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 10 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 11 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 11 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 10 PM"]
Description: Quaint cafe/bar in the McGill Rose Garden with hot drinks, craft beer & wines, plus photo sessions.
Comments:
Quaint, rustic, herbal, and full of character. It feels lived in and well loved, all adding up to a marvelously inviting place that I cannot recommend enough. It took me a few years of living in Charlotte and hearing people mention it before I finally made my way here, and I’m so glad I did.

This is top tier, 100 out of 10, for remote workers or students who feel stressed and need a spot where they can open a laptop, get some work done, then step away and relax. The McGill Rose Garden here provides just that. It's much larger than it looks from the road and gives you a forest-like, nature-filled oasis in the middle of one of Charlotte’s busiest neighborhoods.

The garden is the draw here. Don’t come just to sit inside. Bring a book, bring a friend, bring your dog. Show up early to beat the heat, or if it's not that hot, show up whenever. There is plenty of space to spread out and find your own quiet little corner.

It’s also a top tier date spot. The garden is large enough that, outside of peak weekend hours, you're likely to find a tucked-away space to sit together and have an intimate conversation. I went on a weekday morning and have never felt more at ease anywhere in Charlotte while working remotely. 

Indoor seating is limited, but if you show up early, you can usually get a spot. And every now and then, stand up, stretch your legs, and walk the garden paths. What a lovely place this is.

Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 2 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 2 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 2 PM, 5 PM - 12 AM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 2 PM, 5 PM - 12 AM", "Friday: 8 AM - 2 PM, 5 PM - 12 AM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 12 AM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 11 PM"]
Description:
Coffee by day, jazz by night. Welcome to 88's, where sophistication meets rhythm in the heart of NoDa's newest development. By day we're an upscale coffee haven, offering a carefully curated selection of coffee to start your morning right. 

When the sun sets, we transform into a refined jazz bar, serving classics and favorites, while showcasing Charlottes best groove and blues artists. Named after the 88 keys on a piano, 88's is designed to capture the essence of timeless elegance and warm hospitality.
Comments:
This place could definitely be a set for a La La Land-style movie, but set in Charlotte. Idk what you'd call that, "Transplant Land"? Anyways, this place has a very cool aesthetic. During the day it's a coffee shop, and at night it's a jazz lounge.

Lots of seating, especially if you're going during coffee shop hours. You're guaranteed to find a place to sit, either indoors or outdoors. All the chairs are comfortable too, none of those hard metal chairs most coffee shops use. Every seat here has some plush to it, so your back and butt won't hurt hanging around.

Great access to outlets by the bar and from any seating location that runs along a wall. I feel like this is an underrated remote work spot. They close at 2 PM to flip the vibe daily to jazz lounge and reopen at 5 PM, and at that point it's not really a place to be camped out on your laptop, but it's still very cool.

They have all sorts of events during the week. Some require a ticket, so check their website to be sure. Also, it's right by Queen City Art, formerly The Charlotte Art League, and The Independent Picture House, so you can browse local art installations and/or catch an indie movie after your time here.

A highly artistic and creative corner of the city this is. I love it!

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: 9 AM - 4 PM", "Wednesday: 9 AM - 4 PM", "Thursday: 9 AM - 4 PM", "Friday: 9 AM - 4 PM", "Saturday: 9 AM - 4 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Specialty coffee shop offering a bistro menu, crafted gifts, wine room, & a bit of New England finds. Private & corporate catering options. We also boast a beautiful event space at the shop for corporate dinners, gatherings, baby showers, wedding rehearsal dinners, birthdays, and more.
Comments:
I'll start with what's most important. They have cinnamon rolls, and they are gargantuan. Colossal. Titanic, even. Not in height, but in circumference. I have never seen a cinnamon roll like it. Generously covered in cream cheese frosting and perfectly round. So very round. So very rotund. They’re made in house. They also make a variety of other sweet treats and serve food, coffee, and tea. 

As a place to hang out, work remotely, study, or meet up with friends, it’s top tier. Outlet access is solid in a corner with taller chairs, but what I like most is that the primary seating area has a wall that separates it from the rest of the store. There’s got to be some interior design principle that the TikTok feng shui guy would be able to name about why this works so well. It makes you feel cozy and comfortable, and it eliminates visual distractions. There could be 20 people in line and while you might hear them, you won’t see them, which really helps you feel nestled in.

The interior has a very cute, feminine aesthetic with bright colors and pink highlights here and there. They also have a cool-looking wine section and a larger table for groups. Solid spot all around.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 9 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 10 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 3 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 3 PM"]
Description: Daytime hangout serving a simple menu of pastries, sandwiches & coffee made using local ingredients.
Comments:
The food here is marvelous. Super small space, they limit Wi-Fi access on weekends, and in general ask that people limit their stays to 2 hours or less to free up seating. But I’ve found that on the choice weekdays they're open later, after 4 PM it's pretty quiet, so you can stay a bit longer. Solid evening study/work spot.

Very relaxed vibes. Kind of a Spanish villa aesthetic going on. I don’t drink but this feels like a place you’d drink wine at. Parking is limited out front but drive around the complex and you might find a spot. There’s a garage too nearby with some spots.

Hours: ["Monday: Closed", "Tuesday: Closed", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 6 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 6 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 6 PM", "Saturday: 9 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Embedded in the Twenty Two West studio and storefront is our cozy Chai Window, where we serve up cups of beautifully spiced, rich organic tea made in house from whole spices, velvety oat milk, and softly sweetened with coconut sugar. Each sip adds a layer of warmth, creating a true sip and shop experience!
Comments:
If there were an award for best smelling third place in Charlotte, this place would win. The chai smells marvelous, but also the locally made candles, incense sticks, and various other herbal, earthy, homey items that are sold in the same building by the store Twenty Two West.

The space is in a converted home which is mostly for Twenty Two West but The Chai Window has a couch, a table with three chairs, and some other tables here and there. So, it’s small. Super small, but charming, adorable even.

Don’t come here with your laptop to work or study, that doesn’t match the vibe. Just relax. Breathe in the pleasant smelling air. Peruse the gift shop. Sip chai. Kick back. Buy some incense. The Serenity Now blend with lavender, frankincense, and clove is wonderful.

Hours: ["Monday: 11 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 11 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 11 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 11 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 11 AM - 8:30 PM", "Saturday: 11 AM - 8:30 PM", "Sunday: 11 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: Bubble tea shop containing a variety of selections including hot/cold drinks, coffee & slushes.
Comments: If you’re a tea person, you’ll clock the smell of Oolong the moment you walk in. A lot of their cold drinks are also available hot and they clearly mark that on the menu with an H, which I appreciate. Also, it’s right next to Super G Mart, great Asian food/grocery store.

Address: 101 W Worthington Ave Suite 150, Charlotte, NC 28203
Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 9 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 8 PM"]
Description: Italian Bakery & Coffee serving Tiramisu, Sfogliatelle, Lobster Tail, Cannoli, Focaccia, Croissant, Eclair Gluten Free, Zeppole di San Giuseppe.
Comments:
Most of the seating here is outside. The interior is tiny, just a couch or two, but the outdoor tables sit along a narrow walkway that feels tucked away from the bustle of South End. Croissants are solid.

If you are coming from outside South End, aim for a warm day because you will probably be sitting outdoors. Bitty and Beau’s and Not Just Coffee are around the corner, so you can easily walk to either spot.

Pro tip, park in the Atherton Mill lot for free and walk over.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 6 PM"]
Description: Family-owned Ethiopian/Eritrean coffee shop bringing authentic East African coffee culture to Matthews. Known for homemade tiramisu, traditional coffee, a lineup of pastries, unique croissant sandwiches, and warm hospitality!
Comments:
New as of 2025. Some nice artwork on the walls, including Ethiopian and Eritrean-inspired pieces. The owners are very kind and welcoming. They even have a note on the window inviting remote workers to hang out for a while. Croissant sandwiches, excellent coffee (Ethiopia is the birthplace of coffee, so they know their stuff), tea, pastries (including cinnamon rolls 💯🙌), ice cream🍦, and snack items. They've got it all!

It is a smaller space, but it's well laid out. Good outlet access, and most seating runs along a wall, so you have something solid behind you. Sitting in the middle of foot traffic feels unsettling to me, so I appreciate that layout. There's even a little outdoor seating.

I volunteer with a nonprofit called the Carolina Youth Coalition (great organization, worth checking out if you're looking for volunteer opportunities), and many of the students are Ethiopian or Eritrean. If this spot were closer to them, I could see it becoming a popular after-school hangout. It's a true third place!

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 12 PM, 3 PM - 9:30 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 9:30 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 9:30 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 9:30 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 10:30 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 10:30 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Quaint little coffee shop offering craft and domestic beers on tap as well as in bottles and cans. Also offering wine and mimosas.

Address: 830 E King St, Kings Mountain, NC 28086
Hours: ["Monday: 6 AM - 2 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 2 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 2 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 2 PM", "Friday: 6 AM - 2 PM", "Saturday: 6 AM - 2 PM", "Sunday: 6 AM - 2 PM"]
Description: Friendly neighborhood coffee shop serving quality coffee in a welcoming, community-focused space. Perfect for studying, catching up with friends, or relaxing on the wraparound porch. Everyone is always welcome!
Comments:
If you're headed to Crowder's Mountain State Park for some hiking, definitely stop here as well. Quaint, homey spot, and they have cinnamon rolls! It's owned by Nakisha Wenzel, who has a great story behind opening the shop, which you can read here. 

Plenty of seating, including rocking chairs on a porch that are great for reading a book in. The whole place has cabin vibes. There's comfy couches, rocking chairs, armchairs, wooden chairs, bar style seats, every kind of seating you need. If you love Christmas, there's a year round Christmas tree so you can always stay in the holiday spirit. There's a bookshelf as well if you want to grab something to read.

There's coffee and tea, but also pastries, sandwiches, and waffles so you can stay fed. Free Wi-Fi so also a great place to get some work done or study. One of the cutest cafes in greater Charlotte that I hardly hear anyone mention. Awesome spot!

Hours: ["Monday: 6 AM - 4 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 4 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 4 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 4 PM", "Friday: 6 AM - 4 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: Cozy boutique coffee shop known for canned iced lattes and creative specialty drinks. Started as a coffee trailer, now a permanent spot in downtown Dallas. Friendly service, unique flavors, and free street parking. Welcoming local atmosphere for coffee lovers and friends.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 2 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 2 PM"]
Description: We serve non-profit Colombian coffee, delicious pastries, artisan gelato, and grab & go food. Our focus is on outreach and serving the community.
Comments:
Calming, serene, clean vibes. Plenty of seating, and it’s spread out nicely. It doesn’t feel cramped at all. They make excellent use of what is a pretty large space for a coffee shop. Most of the seating is against a wall, so you’ve got something solid on one side to anchor you. There’s probably some kind of psychology behind how sitting with a wall nearby makes you feel safer, cozier, more nestled in. It’s newer and spotless as a result. Immaculate, really.

It’s inside Myers Park United Methodist Church, but it’s its own thing. Manolo’s is a Latin bakery in Charlotte, and this is their expansion into the coffee shop world. It’s their space, but it's clearly faith friendly and loved by the church.

Top tier spot to get some work done. They close in the early afternoon though, so plan accordingly. Seating is placed smartly with good access to outlets. There's artwork everywhere, and you’ll find outlets in most corners if you look. There’s also a long table down a hallway for group work and even a bookable conference room. Fast and free Wi-Fi. Gelato, pastries, coffee, and what makes me happiest, loose leaf herbal teas. Not bagged. Properly steeped. The Cherry Green is my favorite.

Parking can get tight during peak hours since it shares space with the church, but there’s overflow parking across the street. And a bonus, the Harris Teeter near here is two levels and has a huge patio on the second floor. It runs the length of the building and has plenty of seating and a wine bar. It’s popular with Myers Park soccer moms and book clubs, but I’ve posted up there solo to read a few times. A hidden gem.

Hours: ["Monday: 9 AM - 12 AM", "Tuesday: 9 AM - 12 AM", "Wednesday: 9 AM - 12 AM", "Thursday: 9 AM - 1 AM", "Friday: 9 AM - 2 AM", "Saturday: 9 AM - 2 AM", "Sunday: 9 AM - 12 AM"]
Description:
Charlotte's most lush cocktail bar is now an all day cafe in the heart of South End. Start in the morning with coffee and espresso by local roaster Night Swim Coffee—plus flavored syrups and mushroom-based wellness shots—paired with savory and sweet breakfast bites by Built On Hospitality executive Chef Chris Coleman.

The Noon & Night menu features bold, craveable flavors for lunch, dinner, and everything in between (and after). Cocktail service begins at 10 a.m. on weekends and 2 p.m. on weekdays. Enjoy a Bob Peters creation with your brunch, afternoon calls, or when the workday is done. Reservations accepted and 21+ after 5pm, daily.
Comments:
Used to just be a cocktail bar, but expanded in 2025 into an all-day café. Breakfast, lunch, coffee and tea (from Night Swim), smoothies, alcohol, and more. Earthy, floral, elegant vibes. If you're into plants, you'll love this place. They're everywhere, and every single one is real. I went in the morning once to work, and a gardener came through to water them all.

It really is a beautiful space. Great natural lighting pours in through the roof windows, and on a sunny day, it looks gorgeous. The couches are super comfortable, and one of them sits right next to a stone platform with a constant little waterfall trickling through the plants. It's peaceful and makes the whole place feel alive. I really enjoyed working from here. It wasn't busy at all during the day, and access to outlets is outstanding, 10/10.

Right around the corner, you've got Wooden Robot and Babaloo Coffee Club in walking distance, both third places in their own right. PIE.ZAA is also right next door to this place, and they sell pizza by the slice. If you've had Zämbies or Benny Pennello's, it's similar to those, and pretty good.

Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 4 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 4 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 4 PM"]
Description: In the eclectic South End neighborhood, Babaloo Coffee Club invites you to indulge in a world of exquisite flavors. Step inside and be greeted by the rich aroma of freshly brewed coffee, enticing sandwiches, handcrafted empanadas, and delectable seasonal sweets. Unwind in a warm and inviting ambiance, perfect for catching up with friends. Babaloo Coffee Club is your go-to destination for a delightful sensory journey that celebrates the art of coffee
Comments:
I nearly introduced a "Tiny" sizing to this website after visiting this spot. It's beyond small, it's micro. There are quite literally two tables, totaling four seats, inside, with a handful more outside. If you're a morning person, this is a marvelous spot to bring a book, grab a coffee, and have your pick of the place before it gets busy. It feels closer to a grab-and-go stall than a place to hang out a while, but there is seating, it's just limited.

If you're waiting for me to comment about the quality of the coffee, I'm sorry. I'm not a coffee person. This website has just about every coffee shop in greater Charlotte and yet its creator does not drink coffee. I'm an herbal tea guy. Hot, specifically.

They had some tea options, Harney & Sons, which is about as good as it gets for bagged teas. I think the coffee is good though, because a lot of people told me to go here thinking I might enjoy it. I go to coffee shops for the vibes, the aesthetic, the atmosphere, the ambience. The coffee could be terrible and I'd be none the wiser, so long as I can nestle into a corner, flip open my laptop, and hang out.

Description: Vicente is a bakery and open kitchen where guests can watch pastries and breads made from scratch, blending science, artistry, and tradition. Inspired by Venezuelan, North American, and Buenos Aires roots, the menu offers globally inspired treats with familiar, comforting flavors.
Comments:
Small spot, maybe not ideal for an extended stay, but perfect for grabbing a bite or meeting a friend for a chat. There’s outdoor seating so on warm days finding a seat isn’t hard. There’s also Wi-Fi so you’re welcome to hangout a while if you want. Just be warned, inside it’s all high chairs, so not much back support.

But let’s talk about what’s most important at a bakery, the pastries. They're known for their croissants so definitely get one if you go. Then we have the focaccia or croissant breakfast sandwich, expensive ($11 when last I went), but good. All of that though is secondary to their cinnamon rolls. They call them coffee rolls, but I protest that label. Let’s dissect.

A coffee roll is basically a yeast doughnut ring that is fried, coated in a sugar glaze, and rarely holds much cinnamon. A true cinnamon roll is baked, shaped into a spiral with cinnamon-sugar folded between the layers, and usually finished with a cream cheese or vanilla icing. Completely different textures and flavor profile.

They’re similar enough that most people call them the same thing, but they’re different. What Vicente sells are cinnamon rolls. Both the icing and the cinnamon sugar are flavored with a hint of coffee (hit or miss on if you'll like this, I didn't), hence their decision to call it a coffee roll, but it's a cinnamon roll. Trust me on this, cinnamon rolls are my specialty. 

Finally, the staff here is very kind. A coworker of mine got into an accident right in front of the store, and they came out to check on them, made sure they were okay, and gave them a free box of pastries. Truly run by great people!

Description: Chief’s Modern Cocktail Parlor is a cocktail lounge in a historic NoDa mill home with beverages crafted by the one and only, Bob Peters. In the morning, we serve coffee and espresso, sourced from local Charlotte roaster, Night Swim Coffee, with light breakfast items from our sister restaurant, The Goodyear House.

I (the person who runs this website) don't drink, so I'm not often found at cocktail bars, but I've heard nothing but good things about this place. If you end up going contact me so I can update this comment with your take.

Place: The Holy Grind Coffee House of Kannapolis
Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 8 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description: A family-owned, Christian-based coffee house in Kannapolis serving coffee, espresso drinks, teas, smoothies, frozen drinks, pastries, bakery items, breakfast options, sandwiches, and light lunch items in a calm neighborhood café setting. The space has a warm and relaxing atmosphere with uplifting music, indoor seating, encouraging details, and a drive-through for quick pickup. The menu includes hot and cold coffee drinks, specialty beverages, muffins, breakfast burritos, flatbreads, chicken salad croissants, pastries, and seasonal drinks. Good for coffee, breakfast, lunch, studying, casual conversation, live music evenings, and a relaxed community-focused café visit.
Comments:
I’m biased because I’m a Christian, but I love this place and how much it embraces faith in the decor. There are Bible verses on the tables, Bible verses on a TV with a rotating slideshow of information, a community Bible with a cup of highlighters where visitors are encouraged to highlight their favorite verses, and a shelf with Christian literature on it.

And they have cinnamon rolls, which are second only to Jesus (I'm joking calm down Pharisees) in the list of things I love. The icing on their cinnamon rolls tastes like vanilla ice cream. When warmed it doesn't melt off the sides, but when you bite into it the icing starts to melt inwardly into the roll. It's lovely. A top tier cinnamon roll, made with love and the Holy Spirit I'm sure, hallelujah.

I don’t drink coffee, so I can’t tell you how it tastes. I’m sure it’s good, but they also have tea and cold drinks. The lavender lemonade is great, and then they have sandwiches and various other pastries. Everything you need. Free Wi-Fi, solid access to outlets, it’s all good.

Aesthetically, it’s bright with windows on all sides, clean and modern in a cozy way. So instead of metal chairs, they have wicker basket style ones, then a section with couches and armchairs, and another corner with 2 large beanbag chairs. There are a few big tables for groups, bar-style high chair seating for individuals, and a handful of smaller tables with chairs. Enough space for everyone to find a corner. It’s not a huge space, but they use every corner of it well.

Place: The Holy Grind Coffee House of China Grove
Address: 819 S Main St, China Grove, NC 28023
Hours: ["Monday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Tuesday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Wednesday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Thursday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Friday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 7 PM", "Sunday: Closed"]
Description:
The Holy Grind Coffee House is a cozy coffee shop dedicated to serving premium, ethically-sourced coffee in a welcoming, community-focused environment. Our expertly crafted beverages, from classic espresso drinks to innovative seasonal specialties, are paired with a selection of baked goods and healthy snacks. 

We blend faith, quality, and sustainability; striving to create a space where customers can enjoy both great coffee and meaningful connections. With free Wi-Fi, cozy seating, and a passion for fostering healthy communities, we cater to everyone—from students and remote workers to coffee enthusiasts.
Comments:
Cozy. A coffee shop worthy of the often overused adjective cozy. On my first visit, I saw a group of what looked like longtime friends seated in an area with couches, armchairs, loveseats, and everything they needed to nestle in and catch up with each other. It’s overwhelmingly clear you’re invited to hang out a while here. The setup is inviting.

There’s some bar-style seating with great access to outlets along a wall for remote work and studying, then a few more tables interspersed throughout the space, plus an outdoor patio. It really does feel like someone’s extended living room in the best way possible. And they have cinnamon rolls. Marvelous cinnamon rolls. In addition to all sorts of drinks and food and, of course, coffee, which I don’t drink, but I’m sure is good.

Description: Sip City Market & Bottle Shop is Charlotte’s go-to destination for craft beer, fine wine, and non-alcoholic beverages—plus a versatile event space perfect for private parties, birthday celebrations, bridal showers, game nights, and more. Located in the heart of the city, we offer build-your-own 6-packs, rotating beer and wine tastings, and a welcoming community vibe. Open late every night, Sip City combines a curated drink selection with a unique venue for unforgettable experiences.
Comments:
Daily Ritual Coffee is also in this same space. So, it just depends on when you go, whether it's Daily Ritual or Sip City, same building either way. 

The vibe is creative and cozy, like a mini art gallery, with paintings from local artists on the walls and various card games on the tables. Most of the seating is four-person tables, but there are comfy couches mixed in and a bit of outdoor seating.

Good outlet access, and good tea options, including pure Japanese matcha. It’s right across the street from Central Coffee, a Charlotte staple that’s fantastic but super small. If you’re in the area and need more space, this is a great alternative. Turn into the lot next to Two Scoops Creamery for parking, spots are limited though.

Description: Upscale café and bakery featuring French desserts, pastries, coffee and tea. Custom cake orders and gourmet gifts.
Comments: Excellent presentation, small and cozy place, quite expensive though.

Address: 300 Technology Center Way Suite 200, Rock Hill, SC 29730
Hours: ["Monday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Tuesday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Wednesday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Thursday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Friday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Saturday: 8 AM - 5 PM", "Sunday: 8 AM - 5 PM"]
Description: Experience coffee like never before at Techno Café, where our robotic barista crafts premium espresso drinks with precision. Our stunning industrial space features soaring 20-foot columns, floor-to-ceiling windows, and multiple seating areas perfect for working, meeting, or relaxing. Enjoy locally-made pastries in our quiet, welcoming atmosphere. Free parking available in garage. Come see why we're Rock Hill's must-visit coffee destination!
Comments:
A fully automated coffee shop. No baristas, just robots making the drinks. My first reaction? That sounds soulless, heartless, anti-human. Like it's putting baristas out of work and killing one of the main reasons people go to coffee shops in the first place. The vibes, the human interaction, the sense of community that makes a third place feel alive. But the owners kept all the right things. Cozy interior design, a good amount of seating, and little nooks and corners to settle into. They just automated the barista and ordering part. It's still a warm, comfortable place to hang out. It's just that the drinks aren't "made with love" so much as they're "made with precision."

The shop sits inside the Rock Hill Event Center, which has a restaurant and all sorts of things going on, including the American Cornhole League Headquarters. Walk around the entire building if you visit. There's a very cool history of Rock Hill on display, including a model train set telling the story of the building before it became an event space. The whole thing makes for a great day trip from Charlotte.

The café itself isn't super huge, but every corner has seating and there's good access to outlets. It is a great spot for working remotely. There's a cute, artsy corner with half walls that's perfect for focusing. No hot tea from the machine, which is my thing, but all the coffee options are there. Pastries from local bakers (Wild Flour Sweets and The Daily Dough), snacks, and even store merch are available from vending machines.

I'm not a coffee person, so I can't speak to the quality of the brews, but everyone there seemed satisfied. Empire Pizza, right across from the building, is said to be pretty good too. If you're reading this, definitely check it out and form your own opinion.

Description: Join us for locally roasted coffee, cold brew, a full range of expert espresso beverages, and seasonal drinks! We serve fresh pastries, unique bagel creations, original cocktails, beer, and wine. Our coffee is roasted locally in Rock Hill, SC, and beans are available onsite or via our website for retail purchase. Visit our website for more information. Better yet, drop by to see us in person!
Comments: It's super small, but it’s in “The Alley at Latta Arcade”, a larger business building with seating lining the hallways, so there's spots to plop down and hangout. Not really a great location to work from, more like grab a coffee and read a book. The seating is mostly high chairs and tables, no outlet access. The cinnamon rolls they sell are incredible though.

Comments: Cool, hip, bar with a market, food, and some seating meant for people to post up for a while. Have seen a few students studying here, and some remote workers. It’s around the corner from Waterbean NoDa, so another good option close by.

Hours: ["Monday: 6 AM - 5 PM", "Tuesday: 6 AM - 5 PM", "Wednesday: 6 AM - 5 PM", "Thursday: 6 AM - 5 PM", "Friday: 6 AM - 5 PM", "Saturday: 7 AM - 6 PM", "Sunday: 7 AM - 6 PM"]

Comments:
Very small, seating for maybe 8ish people at max, and that'd be a stretch. Certainly not a place to go if you're looking to study/work and be guaranteed a spot. It's very nice though, a newer location with all the menu options that make Caribou an all-around better choice than Starbucks (to me at least). 

If you're in Monroe from Charlotte or one of the other surrounding areas, don't bother coming here. Go to August Lily Florist, High Octane Coffee, or Alice Jules Coffee House instead. All locally owned, all unique in their own ways, and all far more worthy of a visit in Monroe than Caribou, given it's status as a national chain.
